.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/19--feature-flag-reference/01-1-search-pipeline-features-speckit.md:3:description: "Feature flag reference for the Spec Kit Memory search pipeline controls, compatibility shims, and retrieval-shaping toggles."
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/19--feature-flag-reference/01-1-search-pipeline-features-speckit.md:31:| `SPECKIT_CAUSAL_BOOST` | `true` | boolean | `lib/search/causal-boost.ts` | Enables causal-neighbor graph boosting. Top seed results (up to 25% of result set, capped at 5) walk the causal edge graph up to 2 hops, applying a per-hop boost capped at 0.05. Combined causal + session boost ceiling is 0.20. |
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/19--feature-flag-reference/01-1-search-pipeline-features-speckit.md:43:| `SPECKIT_CONTEXT_HEADERS` | `true` | boolean | `lib/search/hybrid-search.ts` | **IMPLEMENTED (Sprint 019).** P1-4: Contextual tree injection into returned chunks. When enabled, `injectContextualTree()` string-prepends hierarchical context headers (`[parent > child — description]`, max 100 chars) using PI-B3 cached spec folder descriptions. Token budget adjusted for calibrated header overhead (~26 tokens/result) before truncation (CHK-060). |
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/19--feature-flag-reference/01-1-search-pipeline-features-speckit.md:61:| `SPECKIT_FOLDER_DISCOVERY` | `true` | boolean | `lib/search/search-flags.ts` | PI-B3: automatic spec folder discovery. Matches the query against cached one-sentence folder descriptions to identify the most relevant spec folder without triggering full-corpus search. Discovery failure is non-fatal. |
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/19--feature-flag-reference/01-1-search-pipeline-features-speckit.md:67:| `SPECKIT_GRAPH_SIGNALS` | `true` | boolean | `lib/search/search-flags.ts` | Enables the broader Stage 2 graph-signal surface. When active, graph momentum and causal-depth scoring remain available and the graph-walk rollout flag can graduate from `off` to `trace_only` or `bounded_runtime`. |
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/19--feature-flag-reference/01-1-search-pipeline-features-speckit.md:68:| `SPECKIT_GRAPH_WALK_ROLLOUT` | inherited from `SPECKIT_GRAPH_SIGNALS` | enum (`off`, `trace_only`, `bounded_runtime`) | `lib/search/search-flags.ts` | Controls the bounded graph-walk ladder. `off` disables the walk bonus, `trace_only` keeps rollout state and diagnostics visible with zero applied bonus, and `bounded_runtime` applies the capped Stage 2 graph-walk bonus while preserving deterministic ordering protections. Unsupported values do not create hidden extra modes: the resolver falls back to `bounded_runtime` when graph signals are enabled, otherwise `off`. |
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/19--feature-flag-reference/01-1-search-pipeline-features-speckit.md:100:| `SPECKIT_RESPONSE_TRACE` | `false` | boolean | `handlers/memory-search.ts` | **IMPLEMENTED (Sprint 019).** P0-2: Include provenance data (scores, source, trace) in `memory_search` response envelopes. Opt-in via `includeTrace: true` parameter. The trace surface now also carries `sessionTransition` and bounded graph-walk diagnostics when those signals are active. Also propagates through `memory_context` when `includeTrace` is forwarded to internal search calls (CHK-040). When disabled, response format is unchanged (backward compatible). |

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.3.0.1.md:11:Every memory save still called the full `runEntityLinking(...)` pipeline. That meant one new memory could trigger a corpus-wide entity-linking scan even when only the saved memory's new entity set needed cross-folder linking. Save-time linking now uses a memory-scoped incremental path. The linker resolves entities for the saved memory, expands them through `entity_catalog` aliases, finds only matching memories in other spec folders, and creates just those cross-document edges. The full linker still exists as the broad fallback path, but normal save-time work is now bounded to the changed memory.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.3.0.1.md:21:The new incremental linker path needed proof that it stayed narrowly scoped and still handled alias-normalized entity matches like `TF-IDF` versus `tf idf`. Added focused regression tests covering memory-scoped match discovery, alias-aware incremental linking, and save-scope edge creation so the optimized path is guarded independently of the older full-linking pipeline.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.3.0.1.md:41:| `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/entity-linker.vitest.ts` | Added regression coverage for scoped incremental linking and alias-aware entity matching |

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.0.0.0.md:1:This is the release that turns the spec kit from a collection of scripts and tools into a full documentation and memory platform. It completes a 19-phase program that rebuilt how the system stores, searches, and retrieves memories -- and then aligned every piece of documentation, every agent definition, and every command description to match what was actually built. The goal is to make the workflow easier to follow and easier to trust.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/structure/folder_routing.md:101:> **Explicit-target priority.** When the caller passes an explicit `[spec-folder]` CLI argument, that target is authoritative — it always wins over auto-detect, alignment scoring, and any heuristic preference. At a phase parent specifically, this means a save invoked with the parent path runs the parent-level pointer-write path (`derived.last_active_child_id = null` + `derived.last_active_at = now`); a save invoked with an explicit child path bubbles up the child's `packet_id` into the parent's pointer. Re-run with the explicit target you actually want if a save routed to the wrong folder.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/22--context-preservation/16-mcp-auto-priming.md:12:The spec-doc record-surface hook tracks session priming state. On the first tool call of any session, it assembles a PrimePackage with constitutional memories, code graph status snapshot, and any triggered memories from the current prompt. This package is injected into the MCP response hints, giving the AI runtime immediate context without requiring an explicit memory_context call. Subsequent tool calls skip priming (one-shot behavior). Priming status is exposed via session_health. Priming is now session-scoped via a Set<string> of primed session IDs rather than a process-global boolean, correctly handling multiple concurrent sessions.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/structure/phase_definitions.md:79:A phase parent only requires **the lean trio** at the parent level: `spec.md`, `description.json`, `graph-metadata.json`. Heavy docs (`plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `checklist.md`, `decision-record.md`, `implementation-summary.md`) live exclusively in the phase children where they stay accurate to that phase's actual work. The parent's `spec.md` carries the Phase Documentation Map; the parent's `graph-metadata.json` carries the structured rollup including `derived.last_active_child_id` and `derived.last_active_at` pointer fields:
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/structure/phase_definitions.md:84:├── description.json           # Discovery metadata
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/structure/phase_definitions.md:85:├── graph-metadata.json        # Children list + derived rollup + last_active_child_id pointer
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/structure/phase_definitions.md:88:│   ├── description.json
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/structure/phase_definitions.md:89:│   ├── graph-metadata.json
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/structure/phase_definitions.md:99:**Detection rule (single source of truth):** A spec folder is a phase parent iff (a) it has at least one direct child matching `^[0-9]{3}-[a-z0-9-]+$` AND (b) at least one such child has `spec.md` OR `description.json`. The shell helper `is_phase_parent()` (in `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/shell-common.sh`) and the ESM JS helper `isPhaseParent()` (in `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/spec/is-phase-parent.js`) both implement this contract; they MUST agree on every input. The validator's phase-parent branches in `check-files.sh`, `check-level-match.sh`, `check-anchors.sh`, `check-section-counts.sh`, and `check-template-headers.sh` skip Level-N expectations when this returns true.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/26-core-workflow-infrastructure.md:3:description: "Shared workflow modules that load configuration, gate indexing, score memory quality, build titles and topics, edit frontmatter, review post-save output, and persist indexed memories."
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/26-core-workflow-infrastructure.md:18:`config.ts` is the central configuration loader. It discovers the scripts root by walking upward until it finds `package.json`, loads `config.jsonc`, strips JSONC comments before parsing, validates every merged field against safe defaults, auto-converts legacy `qualityAbortThreshold` values on a `1-100` scale into canonical `0.0-1.0`, freezes the static config surface, and exports helpers for locating active `specs/` and `.opencode/specs/` directories without duplicating real-path aliases.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/26-core-workflow-infrastructure.md:22:`post-save-review.ts` is the JSON-mode safety net that runs after a spec-doc record file is written. It rereads the saved file, parses frontmatter plus the fenced `## MEMORY METADATA` YAML block, and compares the on-disk result against the original payload to catch silent overrides or losses. The review currently checks for generic titles, path-fragment trigger phrases, missing manual trigger phrases, tier mismatches, zero `decision_count` despite supplied decisions, context-type mismatches, and boilerplate descriptions. Findings are graded as `HIGH`, `MEDIUM`, or `LOW`, can be converted into a capped score penalty, and are printed in a machine-readable review block.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/26-core-workflow-infrastructure.md:24:`quality-scorer.ts` computes the pre-index quality score for a generated memory. It scores trigger phrases, key topics, file descriptions with provenance-sensitive trust weighting, content length combined with primary-title specificity, leaked HTML detection, and observation-title deduplication. It then applies contamination penalties, caps low-quality outputs when semantic sufficiency fails, and returns both normalized and percentage-style scores together with warnings, quality flags, dimension breakdowns, and insufficiency metadata.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/10--graph-signal-activation/16-typed-traversal.md:12:When the graph has few edges relative to the number of memories, deep multi-hop traversal is unreliable and expensive. This feature detects sparse graphs (density below 0.5) and automatically constrains the causal boost walk to a single typed hop. For all graphs, it introduces intent-aware traversal that maps query intents (fix_bug, add_feature, find_decision, etc.) to prioritized edge types, so the graph walk follows the most relevant relationships first.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/23-spec-lifecycle-automation.md:22:Spec creation is handled by `create.sh`, which defaults to Level 1 unless `--level` is provided. The script creates `scratch/` and `memory/`, copies the level template set, optionally creates a git branch, writes `description.json` when the Node helper is available, and runs quiet post-create validation unless `SPECKIT_SKIP_POST_VALIDATE=1` is set. .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/31-evaluation-benchmark-and-import-policy-tooling.md:3:description: "Operator-facing eval runners and policy checks that measure retrieval quality, benchmark runtime behavior, and enforce architectural import boundaries for system-spec-kit scripts."
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/31-evaluation-benchmark-and-import-policy-tooling.md:26:`check-architecture-boundaries.ts` is the architecture-policy enforcer for two documented gaps. GAP A walks all source files under `shared/` and rejects imports that reach into `mcp_server/` or `scripts/`, whether by relative path, absolute path, or `@spec-kit/*` package prefix. GAP B scans top-level `mcp_server/scripts/*.ts` wrappers and fails anything that is too large, lacks `child_process` usage, or does not delegate to `scripts/dist/`, which keeps wrapper files thin and prevents business logic from drifting into the compatibility layer.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/31-evaluation-benchmark-and-import-policy-tooling.md:30:`import-policy-rules.ts` is the shared import-policy predicate used by the import-boundary checks in this directory. It normalizes both relative and package-scoped import paths, collapses `..` traversal segments, and flags any path that reaches `@spec-kit/mcp-server/{lib,core,handlers}` or relative `../mcp_server/(lib|core|handlers)` internals. Together with `scripts/evals/README.md`, it encodes the current policy that eval scripts should prefer the public `api/` boundary and use narrow exceptions only through the allowlist workflow.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/10--graph-signal-activation/17-temporal-edges.md:10:`valid_at`/`invalid_at` columns on `causal_edges` enable time-bounded graph traversal, with `invalidateEdge()` marking outdated edges and `getValidEdges()` filtering to currently valid ones. .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:3:description: "Unified reference combining the complete current system feature inventory for the Spec Kit Memory MCP server."
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:74:When no `specFolder` is provided, automatic spec folder discovery attempts to identify the most relevant folder from the query text using a cached one-sentence description per spec folder. If the target folder can be identified from the description alone, the system avoids full-corpus search entirely. Discovery failure is non-fatal and falls through to the standard retrieval path. This feature runs behind the `SPECKIT_FOLDER_DISCOVERY` flag.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:76:Session management is caller-scoped. Passing `sessionId` enables cross-turn deduplication and lets the handler resume an existing working-memory session. If you do not pass `sessionId`, the handler generates an ephemeral UUID for internal bookkeeping for that single call only. In resume mode, `systemPromptContext` is injected only when auto-resume is enabled, the effective mode resolves to `resume` and the caller supplied a reusable `sessionId`. Anonymous calls do not revive prior session context.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:242:If your search does not find good results on the first try, the system automatically tries again with wider criteria instead of giving up. Think of it like asking a store clerk for a specific item. If they cannot find it on the first shelf, they check the back room and then the warehouse. You almost never walk away empty-handed.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:264:The working memory module (`lib/cognitive/working-memory.ts`) captures salient results from tool invocations and stores them as session-scoped attention items. When a retrieval tool returns results, the system extracts key findings and inserts them into the `working_memory` table with an attention score. These extracted items persist across turns within the same session, enabling cross-turn context continuity.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:320:`routeAs` can force any of the 8 categories and preserves the natural router decision for audit when the override crosses a natural `drop`. Router session context now passes `packet_kind` derived from spec metadata first (`type`, `title`, `description`), with parent-phase fallback only when the metadata is silent.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:410:Constitutional and critical tier memories receive extra protection. Unscoped deletion of these tiers is refused outright. You must provide a `specFolder` to delete constitutional or critical memories in bulk. The `skipCheckpoint` speed optimization, which skips the safety checkpoint for faster execution, is also rejected for these tiers. If the checkpoint creation itself fails for constitutional/critical, the entire operation aborts. For lower tiers, a checkpoint failure triggers a warning but the deletion proceeds because the risk of losing deprecated or temporary memories is low.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:610:This tool scans your project folders for new or changed files and adds them to the searchable knowledge base. It is like a librarian walking through the stacks every day to catalog new arrivals and update records for books that have been revised. Files that have not changed are skipped to save time. If a file fails to process, the system remembers and retries it next time.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:618:The scanner discovers files from three sources: constitutional files (`.opencode/skills/*/constitutional/*.md`), canonical spec documents (`spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `checklist.md`, `decision-record.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, `research/research.md`, `handover.md`), and graph metadata files (`graph-metadata.json`). The retired `specs/**/memory/*.md` surface is no longer discovered and the runtime rejects saves into it. Canonical path deduplication prevents the same file from being indexed twice under different paths (the `specs/` vs `.opencode/specs/` symlink problem).
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:1044:**B3: Edge-deletion filter correctness:** `causal-edges.ts` delete path must match edges where either endpoint equals the target spec-doc record ID (`source_id = ? OR target_id = ?`). Regression coverage validates deletion remains scoped to intended source/target rows.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:1444:The baseline path now initializes the eval database beside the context database under test instead of silently writing to the default eval location. That keeps ad-hoc migration and rollout checks scoped to the database actually being evaluated. The path switch is also wrapped in `try/finally`, so even if `initEvalDb()` fails after closing the previous singleton, the prior eval DB handle is restored instead of leaving global eval state clobbered for later calls.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:1484:Constitutional memories are excluded from degree boosting because they already receive top-tier visibility. The channel runs behind the `SPECKIT_DEGREE_BOOST` feature flag with a degree cache that invalidates only on graph mutations, not per query. That cache is now scoped per database instance via `WeakMap<Database.Database, Map<string, number>>`, with `getDegreeCacheForDb(database)` for lookup and `clearDegreeCacheForDb(database)` for explicit invalidation, so scores from one DB can no longer leak into another. When a spec-doc record has zero edges, the channel returns 0 rather than failing.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:1662:After Stage 2 fusion produces a ranked result set, the causal boost module walks the `causal_edges` graph to amplify scores for memories related to top-ranked seed results. Up to 25% of the result set (capped at 5) serves as seed nodes. The graph walk traverses up to 2 hops via a weighted recursive CTE, applying a per-hop base boost capped at 0.05.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:2792:Current detection checks: generic title, path-fragment trigger phrases, importance_tier mismatch, decision_count = 0 when keyDecisions are present, contextType mismatch, and generic description. Each finding is emitted with a severity level: HIGH (data loss or explicit caller intent overridden), MEDIUM (degraded quality that reduces retrieval accuracy), or LOW (advisory observation). The review output is machine-readable so callers and downstream quality monitors can surface actionable per-field failures without parsing prose. Failure findings are reported back to the caller in the save response but do not abort the save unless a HIGH severity finding triggers the configured abort threshold.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/README.md:80:The script sets up the folder, copies the right templates for the chosen level, initializes `description.json`, and prepares the packet docs plus `scratch/` workspace. .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/README.md:101:Every canonical save now refreshes `description.json.lastUpdated` and `graph-metadata.json.derived.*`, so the default `/memory:save` path is no longer a metadata no-op.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/README.md:170:| **Phase Parent** | n/a (control file only) | spec.md, description.json, graph-metadata.json | Folder contains phase children (`[0-9]{3}-name/` subdirs with their own spec.md/description.json) |
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/README.md:184:├── description.json             # Spec identity and memory tracking metadata
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/README.md:197:`generate-context.js` updates the packet's continuity state for `/speckit:resume`, refreshes `description.json.lastUpdated`, and rewrites `graph-metadata.json` derived fields on every canonical save; recovery then rebuilds context from `handover.md`, `_memory.continuity`, and the packet docs.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/README.md:199:**Phase parents** are an exception. When a folder contains phase children (matching `^[0-9]{3}-[a-z0-9-]+$` with their own `spec.md` or `description.json`), the parent only requires the **lean trio**: `spec.md`, `description.json`, `graph-metadata.json`. Heavy docs (`plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `checklist.md`, `decision-record.md`, `implementation-summary.md`) live exclusively in the children where they stay accurate to that phase's actual work. The parent's `spec.md` carries a Phase Documentation Map; the parent's `graph-metadata.json` carries `derived.last_active_child_id` + `derived.last_active_at` pointer fields that the generator atomically updates on every save (parent saves write `null`; child saves bubble up the child's `packet_id`). `/speckit:resume` reads the pointer first when the target is a phase parent. A fresh pointer (<24h) recurses directly into the active child. A stale or missing pointer falls back to listing children with statuses. Detection is a single source of truth: `is_phase_parent()` (shell) and `isPhaseParent()` (ESM JS) MUST agree.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/README.md:424:A `mkdir`-based advisory lock protects `description.json` and `graph-metadata.json` writes during canonical save, so two parallel `/memory:save` calls for the same packet do not race.

   208	function leasePath() {
   209	  return path.join(resolvedDbDir(), PID_FILE_NAME);
   210	}
   211	
   212	function legacyLeasePaths() {
   213	  return [
   214	    path.join(opencodeDir, 'skill', 'system-spec-kit', 'mcp_server', 'database', PID_FILE_NAME),
   215	  ].map(canonicalizePath);
   216	}

   218	function readLeaseFile(filePath = leasePath()) {
   219	  try {
   220	    const raw = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');
   221	    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
   222	    if (typeof parsed.pid === 'number') return parsed;
   223	  } catch {
   224	    // Missing or corrupt lease files are treated as no active lease.
   225	  }
   226	  return null;
   227	}
   228	
   229	function leaseHeldFromFile(filePath, legacyPath = null) {
   230	  const lease = readLeaseFile(filePath);
   231	  if (!lease) return { held: false, ownerPid: null, staleReclaimable: false, startedAt: null, legacyPath };
   232	  const startedAt = lease.startedAt ?? new Date(0).toISOString();
   233	  try {
   234	    process.kill(lease.pid, 0);
   235	    return { held: true, ownerPid: lease.pid, staleReclaimable: false, startedAt, legacyPath };
   236	  } catch (error) {
   237	    if (error.code === 'ESRCH') return { held: false, ownerPid: lease.pid, staleReclaimable: true, startedAt, legacyPath };
   238	    // Mirror skill-advisor parity — EPERM means the process exists but we lack permission (e.g. sandbox); treat as live lease.
   239	    if (error.code === 'EPERM') return { held: true, ownerPid: lease.pid, staleReclaimable: false, startedAt, legacyPath };
   240	    throw error;
   241	  }
   242	}
   243	
   244	function isLeaseHeld() {
   245	  const primary = leaseHeldFromFile(leasePath());
   246	  if (primary.held) return primary;
   247	
   248	  for (const legacyPath of legacyLeasePaths()) {
   249	    if (legacyPath === leasePath()) continue;
   250	    const legacy = leaseHeldFromFile(legacyPath, legacyPath);
   251	    if (legacy.held || legacy.staleReclaimable) return legacy;
   252	  }
   253	
   254	  return primary;
   255	}

   257	function writeLeaseHeldDiagnostic(leaseResult, suffix = '') {
   258	  const startedAt = leaseResult.startedAt ?? new Date(0).toISOString();
   259	  const legacyMarker = leaseResult.legacyPath ? ' (legacy path)' : '';
   260	  process.stdout.write(`LEASE_HELD_BY:${leaseResult.ownerPid} startedAt=${startedAt}${legacyMarker}${suffix}\n`);

   283	async function reapLeaseChildBeforeRespawn(childPid) {
   284	  const liveness = processLiveness(childPid);
   285	  if (liveness === 'unknown-eperm') {
   286	    return { allowed: false, reason: 'child-liveness-unknown-eperm' };
   287	  }
   288	  if (liveness === 'dead') {
   289	    return { allowed: true, reaped: false, reason: 'child-already-dead' };
   290	  }
   291	
   292	  log(`confirmed-dead socket; reaping recorded context-server child pid ${childPid} before respawn`);
   293	  signalProcess(childPid, 'SIGTERM');
   294	  reapProcessTreeGroups(childPid, { signal: signalProcess });
   295	  const exitedAfterTerm = await waitForPidExit(childPid, RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS);
   296	  if (!exitedAfterTerm) {
   297	    log(`context-server child pid ${childPid} exceeded ${RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS}ms reap grace; sending SIGKILL`);
   298	    signalProcess(childPid, 'SIGKILL');
   299	    await waitForPidExit(childPid, 1000);
   300	  }
   301	  return { allowed: true, reaped: true, reason: 'child-reaped' };
   302	}

   304	async function respawnAfterDeadSocket(leaseResult, decision) {
   305	  if (process.env.SPECKIT_BRIDGE_RESPAWN_DISABLED === '1') {
   306	    writeLeaseHeldDiagnostic(leaseResult, ' (dead-socket-respawn-disabled)');
   307	    return { action: 'report', reason: 'respawn-disabled', socketPath: decision.socketPath };
   308	  }
   309	
   310	  const lease = readLeaseFile(leaseResult.legacyPath || leasePath());
   311	  const childPid = lease?.childPid;
   312	  if (!Number.isInteger(childPid) || childPid <= 0) {
   313	    log('confirmed-dead socket but lease has no childPid; phase-006 gate keeps respawn inert');
   314	    writeLeaseHeldDiagnostic(leaseResult, ' (dead-socket-no-child-pid)');
   315	    return { action: 'report', reason: 'missing-child-pid', socketPath: decision.socketPath };
   316	  }
   317	
   318	  let bootstrapLockHeld = false;
   319	  let respawnLock = null;
   320	  try {
   321	    bootstrapLockHeld = await acquireBootstrapLock({ requireLock: true });
   322	    respawnLock = acquireRespawnLockFile();
   323	    if (!respawnLock.acquired) {
   324	      log(`dead-socket respawn skipped; another launcher owns ${rel(respawnLock.path)}`);
   325	      writeLeaseHeldDiagnostic(leaseResult, ' (dead-socket-respawn-owned)');
   326	      return { action: 'report', reason: 'respawn-lock-held', socketPath: decision.socketPath };
   327	    }
   328	
   329	    const currentLease = readLeaseFile(leaseResult.legacyPath || leasePath());
   330	    if (currentLease?.pid !== leaseResult.ownerPid || currentLease?.childPid !== childPid) {
   331	      log('dead-socket respawn skipped; lease changed while waiting for respawn lock');
   332	      writeLeaseHeldDiagnostic(leaseResult, ' (dead-socket-respawn-superseded)');
   333	      return { action: 'report', reason: 'respawn-superseded', socketPath: decision.socketPath };
   334	    }
   335	
   336	    const reapResult = await reapLeaseChildBeforeRespawn(childPid);
   337	    if (!reapResult.allowed) {
   338	      log(`dead-socket respawn skipped; ${reapResult.reason} for childPid=${childPid}`);
   339	      writeLeaseHeldDiagnostic(leaseResult, ` (dead-socket-${reapResult.reason})`);
   340	      return { action: 'report', reason: reapResult.reason, socketPath: decision.socketPath };
   341	    }
   342	
   343	    buildIfNeeded([]);
   344	    leaseStartedAt = new Date().toISOString();
   345	    writeLeaseFile();
   346	    launchServer();
   347	    return { action: 'respawn', reason: 'spawned', socketPath: decision.socketPath };
   348	  } finally {
   349	    releaseRespawnLockFile(respawnLock);

   715	async function acquireBootstrapLock(options = {}) {
   716	  const requireLock = options.requireLock === true;
   717	  fs.mkdirSync(dbDir, { recursive: true });
   718	  const deadline = Date.now() + 120000;
   719	  while (true) {
   720	    try {
   721	      fs.mkdirSync(lockDir);
   722	      return true;
   723	    } catch (error) {
   724	      if (error.code !== 'EEXIST') {
   725	        throw error;
   726	      }
   727	      // Reclaim a stale lockdir left by a SIGKILL'd launcher (mtime-based; the lockdir records no pid),
   728	      // so a crashed holder cannot block a requireLock respawn for the full 120s deadline.
   729	      try {
   730	        const ageMs = Date.now() - fs.statSync(lockDir).mtimeMs;
   731	        if (ageMs > BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_STALE_MS) {
   732	          log(`reclaiming stale bootstrap lock ${rel(lockDir)} (age ${Math.round(ageMs / 1000)}s)`);
   733	          fs.rmSync(lockDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
   734	          continue;
   735	        }
   736	      } catch (statErr) {
   737	        if (statErr.code === 'ENOENT') continue;
   738	        throw statErr;
   739	      }
   740	      if (artifactsReady() && !requireLock) {
   741	        return false;
   742	      }
   743	      if (Date.now() > deadline) {
   744	        throw new Error(`bootstrap lock timed out at ${rel(lockDir)}`);
   745	      }
   746	      await sleep(1000);
   747	    }
   748	  }
   749	}
   750	
   751	function launchServer() {
   752	  if (shouldSkipLaunch(childProcess)) {
   753	    log('launchServer skipped: a context-server child is already running in this launcher process');
   754	    return false;
   755	  }
   756	  if (!crashLoopGuard) crashLoopGuard = createCrashLoopGuard(getCrashLoopConfig());
   757	  if (supervisorRelaunchTimer) {
   758	    clearTimeout(supervisorRelaunchTimer);
   759	    supervisorRelaunchTimer = null;
   760	  }
   761	  const server = path.join(kitDir, 'mcp_server', 'dist', 'context-server.js');
   762	  const nodeArgs = getContextServerNodeArgs();
   763	  childProcess = spawn(process.execPath, [...nodeArgs, server], {
   764	    cwd: root,
   765	    env: process.env,
   766	    stdio: 'inherit',
   767	  });
   768	  const childPid = childProcess.pid;
   769	  writeLeaseFile(childPid);
   770	  startRssWatchdog(childPid);
   771	
   772	  childProcess.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
   773	    if (launcherShutdownInProgress) {
   774	      return;
   775	    }
   776	    const result = superviseChildExit(
   777	      { code, signal, childPid, intentional: false },
   778	      {
   779	        crashLoopGuard,
   780	        clearLease: clearLeaseFile,
   781	        reapProcessGroup: reapProcessTreeGroups,
   782	        mirrorSignal: (exitSignal) => process.kill(process.pid, exitSignal),
   783	        exit: (exitCode) => process.exit(exitCode),
   784	        scheduleRelaunch: (backoffMs) => {
   785	          log(`context-server child exited code=${code ?? 'null'} signal=${signal ?? 'null'}; relaunching in ${backoffMs}ms`);
   786	          supervisorRelaunchTimer = setTimeout(() => launchServer(), backoffMs);
   787	          supervisorRelaunchTimer.unref?.();
   788	        },
   789	      },
   790	    );
   791	    if (result.action === 'give-up') {
   792	      log(`context-server crash loop detected after ${result.deathsInWindow} deaths; clearing lease and mirroring child exit`);
   793	    }
   794	  });
   795	
   796	  childProcess.on('error', (error) => {
   797	    log(error.stack || error.message);
   798	    process.exit(1);
   799	  });
   800	  return true;
   801	}
   802	
   803	async function shutdownLauncherForSignal(signal) {
   804	  if (launcherShutdownInProgress) return;
   805	  launcherShutdownInProgress = true;
   806	  if (rssWatchdogTimer) clearInterval(rssWatchdogTimer);
   807	  if (supervisorRelaunchTimer) clearTimeout(supervisorRelaunchTimer);
   808	  hfControl.clearTimers();
   809	  await hfControl.stopDemandListener();
   810	
   811	  const children = [];
   812	  if (isChildRunning(childProcess)) {
   813	    children.push({ child: childProcess, label: 'context-server' });
   814	  }
   815	  const modelServerChild = hfControl.getChild();
   816	  if (isChildRunning(modelServerChild)) {
   817	    children.push({ child: modelServerChild, label: 'hf-model-server' });
   818	  }
   819	
   820	  if (children.length === 0) {
   821	    clearLeaseFile();
   822	    process.exit(128);
   823	    return;
   824	  }
   825	
   826	  for (const { child, label } of children) {
   827	    if (label === 'hf-model-server') {
   828	      hfControl.reapProcessTree(child.pid);
   829	    }
   830	    child.kill(signal);
   831	  }
   832	
   833	  const exited = await Promise.race([
   834	    Promise.all(children.map(({ child }) => waitForChildExit(child, RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS))).then((results) => results.every(Boolean)),
   835	    sleep(RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS).then(() => false),
   836	  ]);
   837	  if (!exited) {
   838	    for (const { child } of children) {
   839	      if (isChildRunning(child)) child.kill('SIGKILL');
   840	    }
   841	  }
   842	  clearLeaseFile();
   843	  process.exit(128);
   844	}
   845	
   846	function installSignalHandlers() {
   847	  for (const signal of ['SIGINT', 'SIGTERM', 'SIGHUP', 'SIGQUIT']) {
   848	    process.on(signal, () => {
   849	      void shutdownLauncherForSignal(signal).catch((error) => {
   850	        log(error.stack || error.message);
   851	        clearLeaseFile();
   852	        process.exit(128);
   853	      });
   854	    });
   855	  }
   856	  process.on('uncaughtException', (err) => {
   857	    try {
   858	      clearLeaseFile();
   859	    } catch {
   860	      // Preserve default uncaughtException crash behavior.
   861	    }
   862	    throw err;
   863	  });
   864	}
   865	
   866	async function main() {
   867	  loadProjectEnvFiles();
   868	  const started = now();
   869	  const actions = [];
   870	  let lockHeld = false;
   871	
   872	  try {
   873	    installSignalHandlers();
   874	    // Lease cleanup runs unconditionally regardless of child termination path.
   875	    process.on('exit', clearLeaseFile);
   876	    refreshPaths();
   877	
   878	    const strictSingleWriter = !isStrictModeDisabled(process.env.MK_SPEC_MEMORY_STRICT_SINGLE_WRITER);
   879	    if (strictSingleWriter) {
   880	      const leaseResult = isLeaseHeld();
   881	      if (leaseResult.held && !leaseResult.staleReclaimable) {
   882	        await bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld(leaseResult);
   883	        return;
   884	      }
   885	      if (leaseResult.staleReclaimable) {
   886	        log(`staleReclaimed: true${leaseResult.legacyPath ? ' (legacy path)' : ''}`);
   887	      }
   888	    } else {
   889	      log('MK_SPEC_MEMORY_STRICT_SINGLE_WRITER is disabled; skipping lease check');
   890	    }
   891	
   892	    ensureLayout(actions);
   893	    refreshPaths();
   894	    enforceStandaloneCodeGraphDb(actions);
   895	
   896	    lockHeld = await acquireBootstrapLock();
   897	    if (lockHeld) {
   898	      buildIfNeeded(actions);
   899	      writeState({
   900	        command: 'mk-spec-memory-launcher',
   901	        start: started,
   902	        end: now(),
   903	        status: 'ready',
   904	        actions,
   905	        server: rel(path.join(kitDir, 'mcp_server', 'dist', 'context-server.js')),
   906	      });
   907	    }
   908	
   909	    writeLeaseFile();
   910	    const reprobe = readLeaseFile();
   911	    if (!reprobe || reprobe.pid !== process.pid) {
   912	      const startedAt = reprobe?.startedAt ?? new Date(0).toISOString();
   913	      process.stdout.write(`LEASE_HELD_BY:${reprobe ? reprobe.pid : 'unknown'} startedAt=${startedAt}\n`);
   914	      process.exit(0);

   275	      if (parsed && parsed.statusCode >= 200 && parsed.statusCode < 300 && state === 'ready') {
   276	        finish('alive', `health-${state}`, parsed.body);
   277	        return;
   278	      }
   279	      if (parsed && parsed.statusCode >= 200 && parsed.statusCode < 300 && state === 'loading') {
   280	        const loadStartedAt = parsed.body.loadStartedAt;
   281	        const loadProgressAt = parsed.body.loadProgressAt;
   282	        // The loading budget is per model-load attempt; device fallback re-stamps loadProgressAt.
   283	        const loadingMarker = Number.isFinite(loadProgressAt) && loadProgressAt > 0
   284	          ? loadProgressAt
   285	          : loadStartedAt;
   286	        if (Number.isFinite(loadingMarker) && loadingMarker > 0 && nowMs() - loadingMarker > loadingMaxMs) {
   287	          finish('dead', 'loading-wedged');
   288	          return;
   289	        }
   290	        finish('alive', 'health-loading', parsed.body);
   291	        return;
   292	      }
   293	      finish('dead', state === 'error' ? 'health-error' : 'health-not-ready');
   294	    });
   295	    socket.once('error', (error) => {
   296	      finish('dead', error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'socket-error');
   297	    });
   298	    socket.once('close', () => {
   299	      if (!settled && chunks.length === 0) finish('dead', 'closed-before-reply');
   300	    });
   301	  });
   302	}
   303	
   304	async function maybeBridgeLeaseHolder(options) {
   305	  const {
   306	    serviceName,
   307	    leaseResult,
   308	    loggerPrefix,
   309	    dbDir,
   310	    legacyReport,
   311	    probeTimeoutMs,
   312	    connect,
   313	    bridge,
   314	  } = options;
   315	  const startedAt = leaseResult.startedAt ?? new Date(0).toISOString();
   316	  const legacyMarker = leaseResult.legacyPath ? ' (legacy path)' : '';
   317	  const ownerPid = leaseResult.ownerPid;
   318	  const writeLeaseHeld = (suffix = '') => {
   319	    process.stdout.write(`LEASE_HELD_BY:${ownerPid} startedAt=${startedAt}${legacyMarker}${suffix}\n`);
   320	  };
   321	
   322	  if (process.env.SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_BRIDGE_DISABLED === '1') {
   323	    if (legacyReport) {
   324	      legacyReport(leaseResult);
   325	      return { action: 'report', reason: 'bridge-disabled' };
   326	    }
   327	    writeLeaseHeld();
   328	    return { action: 'report', reason: 'bridge-disabled' };
   329	  }
   330	
   331	  const socketPath = getIpcSocketPath(serviceName, { dbDir });
   332	  if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://') && !fs.existsSync(socketPath)) {
   333	    writeLeaseHeld(' (no-bridge-socket)');
   334	    return { action: 'report', reason: 'no-bridge-socket', socketPath };
   335	  }
   336	
   337	  // deepProbe: REQUIRE a JSON-RPC reply, not just a socket accept. The reap/bridge decision must
   338	  // detect a HUNG daemon (event loop wedged / deadlocked — it still accepts the UDS connection but
   339	  // never services requests). A connect-ok-only probe would bridge a client to the wedged daemon and
   340	  // hang it forever, never respawning. The raised probe timeout (default 5000ms, < the 7000ms launcher
   341	  // grace) already prevents false-reaping a busy-but-responsive daemon mid-FTS-merge, so deep probing
   342	  // does not regress that.
   343	  const probe = await probeDaemon(socketPath, { timeoutMs: probeTimeoutMs, connect, deepProbe: true });
   344	  if (probe.status !== 'alive') {
   345	    process.stderr.write(`[${loggerPrefix}] lease holder pid=${ownerPid} socket=${socketPath} failed liveness probe: ${probe.reason}\n`);
   346	    return { action: 'respawn', reason: probe.reason, socketPath };
   347	  }
   348	
   349	  process.stderr.write(`[${loggerPrefix}] bridging to lease holder pid=${ownerPid} socket=${socketPath}\n`);
   350	  const bridgeToSocket = bridge ?? bridgeStdioToSocket;
   351	  bridgeToSocket(socketPath, {
   352	    onError: () => {
   353	      writeLeaseHeld(' (bridge-refused)');
   354	    },
   355	  });
   356	  return { action: 'bridge', socketPath };
   357	}
   358

   360	  DEFAULT_MODEL_SERVER_LOADING_MAX_MS,

    46	// Load project-local env overrides BEFORE spawning the MCP child. .env.local wins over
    47	// .env, both are gitignored. Existing process.env wins over file values (do not override).
    48	// Minimal parser — no external dependency.
    49	function loadEnvFile(filePath) {
    50	  if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) return 0;
    51	  let count = 0;
    52	  const raw = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');
    53	  for (const line of raw.split('\n')) {
    54	    const trimmed = line.trim();
    55	    if (!trimmed || trimmed.startsWith('#')) continue;
    56	    const m = trimmed.match(/^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*=\s*(.*)$/);
    57	    if (!m) continue;
    58	    const key = m[1];
    59	    let val = m[2];
    60	    // Strip matching outer quotes (single or double)
    61	    if ((val.startsWith('"') && val.endsWith('"')) || (val.startsWith("'") && val.endsWith("'"))) {
    62	      val = val.slice(1, -1);
    63	    }
    64	    if (!(key in process.env)) {
    65	      process.env[key] = val;
    66	      count++;
    67	    }
    68	  }
    69	  return count;
    70	}

    76	}

    49	  const code = error && typeof error === 'object' ? error.code : undefined;
    50	  if (code === 'EPERM' || code === 'EACCES') return true;
    51	  const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error ?? '');

    79	function parseProcessRows(input) {
    80	  if (Array.isArray(input)) {
    81	    return input
    82	      .map((row) => ({
    83	        pid: Number(row.pid),
    84	        ppid: Number(row.ppid),
    85	        rss: Number(row.rss),
    86	      }))
    87	      .filter((row) => Number.isFinite(row.pid) && Number.isFinite(row.ppid) && Number.isFinite(row.rss));
    88	  }
    89	
    90	  return String(input ?? '')
    91	    .split(/\r?\n/)
    92	    .map((line) => line.trim())
    93	    .filter(Boolean)
    94	    .map((line) => {
    95	      const [pid, ppid, rss] = line.split(/\s+/).map((value) => Number.parseInt(value, 10));
    96	      return { pid, ppid, rss };
    97	    })
    98	    .filter((row) => Number.isFinite(row.pid) && Number.isFinite(row.ppid) && Number.isFinite(row.rss));
    99	}
   100	
   101	function defaultProcessRowsRunner() {
   102	  const result = spawnSync('ps', ['-eo', 'pid=,ppid=,rss='], {
   103	    encoding: 'utf8',
   104	    stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
   105	  });
   106	  if (result.error) throw result.error;
   107	  if (result.status !== 0) {
   108	    const error = new Error(result.stderr || `ps exited ${result.status}`);
   109	    error.code = result.status === 1 && /operation not permitted|permission denied/i.test(result.stderr || '') ? 'EPERM' : 'PS_FAILED';
   110	    throw error;
   111	  }
   112	  return result.stdout;
   113	}

   198	function getModelServerIdleConfig(env = process.env) {
   199	  const rawIdleMin = env.SPECKIT_HF_MODEL_SERVER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN;
   200	  const idleMin = rawIdleMin === undefined || rawIdleMin === null || String(rawIdleMin).trim() === ''
   201	    ? 0
   202	    : Number.parseFloat(String(rawIdleMin).trim());
   203	  const enabled = Number.isFinite(idleMin) && idleMin > 0;
   204	  return {
   205	    enabled,
   206	    timeoutMs: enabled ? idleMin * 60000 : 0,
   207	  };
   208	}
   209	
   210	function computeBackoffMs(deathsInWindow, initialBackoffMs, maxBackoffMs) {
   211	  const exponent = Math.max(0, deathsInWindow - 1);
   212	  return Math.min(maxBackoffMs, initialBackoffMs * (2 ** exponent));
   213	}
   214	
   215	function createCrashLoopGuard(options = {}) {
   216	  const maxDeaths = parsePositiveInteger(options.maxDeaths, DEFAULT_CRASH_LOOP_MAX_DEATHS);
   217	  const windowMs = parsePositiveInteger(options.windowMs, DEFAULT_CRASH_LOOP_WINDOW_MS);
   218	  const initialBackoffMs = parsePositiveInteger(options.initialBackoffMs, DEFAULT_CRASH_LOOP_INITIAL_BACKOFF_MS);
   219	  const maxBackoffMs = parsePositiveInteger(options.maxBackoffMs, DEFAULT_CRASH_LOOP_MAX_BACKOFF_MS);
   220	  const nowMs = typeof options.now === 'function' ? options.now : () => Date.now();
   221	  const deaths = [];
   222	
   223	  return {
   224	    recordDeath() {
   225	      const current = nowMs();
   226	      deaths.push(current);
   227	      while (deaths.length > 0 && current - deaths[0] > windowMs) deaths.shift();
   228	      const deathsInWindow = deaths.length;
   229	      return {
   230	        deathsInWindow,
   231	        giveUp: deathsInWindow >= maxDeaths,
   232	        backoffMs: computeBackoffMs(deathsInWindow, initialBackoffMs, maxBackoffMs),
   233	      };
   234	    },
   235	  };
   236	}
   237	
   238	function getCrashLoopConfig(env = process.env) {
   239	  return {
   240	    maxDeaths: parsePositiveInteger(env.SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_CRASH_LOOP_MAX_DEATHS, DEFAULT_CRASH_LOOP_MAX_DEATHS),
   241	    windowMs: parsePositiveInteger(env.SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_CRASH_LOOP_WINDOW_MS, DEFAULT_CRASH_LOOP_WINDOW_MS),
   242	    initialBackoffMs: parsePositiveInteger(env.SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_CRASH_LOOP_INITIAL_BACKOFF_MS, DEFAULT_CRASH_LOOP_INITIAL_BACKOFF_MS),
   243	    maxBackoffMs: parsePositiveInteger(env.SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_CRASH_LOOP_MAX_BACKOFF_MS, DEFAULT_CRASH_LOOP_MAX_BACKOFF_MS),
   244	  };
   245	}
   246	
   247	function getModelServerGiveUpCooldownMs(env = process.env) {
   248	  return parsePositiveInteger(env.SPECKIT_HF_MODEL_SERVER_GIVEUP_COOLDOWN_MS, DEFAULT_MODEL_SERVER_GIVEUP_COOLDOWN_MS);
   249	}
   250	
   251	function superviseChildExit(event, actions) {
   252	  if (event.intentional) {
   253	    actions.clearLease();
   254	    actions.exit(event.code ?? 0);
   255	    return { action: 'intentional-exit' };
   256	  }
   257	
   258	  const decision = actions.crashLoopGuard.recordDeath();
   259	  if (decision.giveUp) {
   260	    actions.clearLease();
   261	    actions.reapProcessGroup(event.childPid);
   262	    if (event.signal) {
   263	      actions.mirrorSignal(event.signal);
   264	    } else {
   265	      actions.exit(event.code ?? 1);
   266	    }
   267	    return { action: 'give-up', deathsInWindow: decision.deathsInWindow };
   268	  }
   269	
   270	  actions.scheduleRelaunch(decision.backoffMs);
   271	  return { action: 'relaunch', deathsInWindow: decision.deathsInWindow, backoffMs: decision.backoffMs };
   272	}
   273	
   274	function processLiveness(pid) {
   275	  if (!Number.isInteger(pid) || pid <= 0) return 'dead';
   276	  try {
   277	    process.kill(pid, 0);
   278	    return 'alive';
   279	  } catch (error) {
   280	    if (error.code === 'ESRCH') return 'dead';
   281	    if (error.code === 'EPERM') return 'unknown-eperm';
   282	    return 'alive';
   283	  }
   284	}
   285	
   286	async function waitForPidExit(pid, timeoutMs) {
   287	  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
   288	  while (Date.now() <= deadline) {
   289	    if (processLiveness(pid) === 'dead') return true;
   290	    await sleep(100);
   291	  }
   292	  return processLiveness(pid) === 'dead';
   293	}
   294	
   295	function signalProcess(pid, signal) {
   296	  try {
   297	    process.kill(pid, signal);
   298	    return true;
   299	  } catch (error) {
   300	    if (error.code === 'ESRCH' || error.code === 'EPERM') return false;
   301	    throw error;
   302	  }
   303	}
   304	
   305	function isChildRunning(child) {
   306	  return child && child.exitCode === null && child.signalCode === null;
   307	}
   308	
   309	function shouldSkipLaunch(child) {
   310	  return Boolean(child && isChildRunning(child));
   311	}
   312	
   313	function refreshDescendantSnapshot(childPid, runner = defaultProcessRowsRunner, options = {}) {
   314	  const snapshot = Array.isArray(options.snapshotPids) ? options.snapshotPids : [];
   315	  const rows = resolveProcessTreeRows(childPid, runner);
   316	  if (rows === null) return snapshot;
   317	  const pids = rows.map((row) => row.pid).filter((pid) => pid !== childPid && pid > 1);
   318	  if (options.snapshot && typeof options.snapshot.set === 'function') {
   319	    options.snapshot.set(pids);
   320	  }
   321	  return pids;
   322	}
   323	
   324	function reapProcessTreeGroups(childPid, options = {}) {
   325	  const runner = options.runner || defaultProcessRowsRunner;
   326	  const signal = options.signal || signalProcess;
   327	  const snapshotPids = Array.isArray(options.snapshotPids) ? options.snapshotPids : [];
   328	
   329	  const liveRows = resolveProcessTreeRows(childPid, runner);
   330	  const liveDescendants = liveRows ? liveRows.map((row) => row.pid).filter((pid) => pid !== childPid) : [];
   331	
   332	  const candidates = [...new Set([...liveDescendants, ...snapshotPids])].filter(
   333	    (pid) => Number.isInteger(pid) && pid > 1 && pid !== childPid && pid !== process.pid && signal(pid, 0),
   334	  );
   335	  if (candidates.length === 0) return;
   336	
   337	  for (const pid of candidates) {
   338	    if (process.platform !== 'win32') signal(-pid, 'SIGTERM');
   339	    signal(pid, 'SIGTERM');
   340	  }

   341	  setTimeout(() => {

   343	      if (process.platform !== 'win32') signal(-pid, 'SIGKILL');
   344	      signal(pid, 'SIGKILL');
   345	    }
   346	  }, 1000).unref();
   347	}

   349	function startRssWatchdog(childPid, options = {}) {
   350	  let fallbackTimer = null;
   351	  const timerState = options.timerState || {
   352	    get: () => fallbackTimer,
   353	    set: (timer) => {
   354	      fallbackTimer = timer;
   355	    },
   356	  };
   357	  const existingTimer = timerState.get();
   358	  if (existingTimer) {
   359	    clearInterval(existingTimer);
   360	    timerState.set(null);
   361	  }
   362	  const logger = options.log || defaultLog;
   363	  const label = options.label || 'context-server';
   364	  const runner = options.runner || defaultProcessRowsRunner;
   365	  let fallbackSnapshotPids = [];
   366	  const snapshot = options.snapshot || {
   367	    set: (pids) => {
   368	      fallbackSnapshotPids = pids;
   369	    },
   370	    get: () => fallbackSnapshotPids,
   371	  };
   372	  const config = options.config || getWatchdogConfig(process.env, logger);
   373	  if (!config.enabled && config.maxRssMb !== null && Number.isFinite(config.maxRssMb) && config.maxRssMb > 0) {
   374	    logger(`RSS watchdog ceiling configured but ${config.selfExitEnv}=1 is not set; breach self-exit remains disabled (descendant tracking still active for crash-loop reap)`);
   375	  }
   376	
   377	  const tickMs = config.enabled ? config.intervalMs : DEFAULT_DESCENDANT_SNAPSHOT_INTERVAL_MS;
   378	  let consecutiveBreaches = 0;
   379	  const timer = setInterval(() => {
   380	    const rows = resolveProcessTreeRows(childPid, runner);
   381	    if (rows === null) {
   382	      consecutiveBreaches = 0;
   383	      return;
   384	    }
   385	    snapshot.set(rows.map((row) => row.pid).filter((pid) => pid !== childPid && pid > 1));
   386	    if (!config.enabled) return;
   387	    const rssMb = rows.reduce((sum, row) => sum + Math.max(0, row.rss), 0) / 1024;
   388	    if (rssMb <= config.maxRssMb) {
   389	      consecutiveBreaches = 0;
   390	      return;
   391	    }
   392	    consecutiveBreaches++;
   393	    logger(`${label} process tree RSS ${rssMb.toFixed(1)} MB exceeds ${config.maxRssMb} MB (${consecutiveBreaches}/${config.consecutiveBreaches})`);
   394	    if (consecutiveBreaches >= config.consecutiveBreaches) {
   395	      const onBreach = options.onBreach || (() => undefined);
   396	      void onBreach(config);
   397	    }
   398	  }, tickMs);
   399	  timer.unref?.();
   400	  timerState.set(timer);
   401	  return timer;
   402	}
   403	
   404	function canonicalizePath(pathValue) {
   405	  const resolvedPath = path.resolve(pathValue);
   406	  try {
   407	    return fs.realpathSync.native(resolvedPath);
   408	  } catch (error) {
   409	    if (error.code === 'ENOENT') return resolvedPath;
   410	    throw error;
   411	  }
   412	}
   413	
   414	function ensureCanonicalDir(dirPath) {
   415	  fs.mkdirSync(canonicalizePath(dirPath), { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
   416	  return canonicalizePath(dirPath);
   417	}
   418	
   419	function normalizeModelServerTarget(value) {
   420	  if (typeof value !== 'string' || value.trim() === '') return null;
   421	  const raw = value.trim();
   422	  if (raw.startsWith('tcp://')) return raw;
   423	  if (raw.startsWith('http://') || raw.startsWith('https://')) {
   424	    const url = new URL(raw);
   425	    return `tcp://${url.hostname}:${url.port || (url.protocol === 'https:' ? '443' : '80')}`;
   426	  }
   427	  if (raw.startsWith('unix://')) return path.resolve(raw.slice('unix://'.length));
   428	  return path.resolve(raw);
   429	}
   430	
   431	function resolveModelServerSocketPath(env = process.env, options = {}) {
   432	  const explicitTarget = normalizeModelServerTarget(options.listenTarget || env.HF_EMBED_SERVER_URL);
   433	  if (explicitTarget) return explicitTarget;
   434	  if (env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR?.startsWith('tcp://')) return env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR;
   435	
   436	  const rawDbDir = typeof options.dbDir === 'function' ? options.dbDir() : options.dbDir;
   437	  const socketDir = env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR
   438	    ? path.resolve(env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR)
   439	    : path.resolve(rawDbDir || path.join(defaultOpencodeDir, 'skills', 'system-spec-kit', 'mcp_server', 'database'));
   440	  return path.join(socketDir, HF_MODEL_SERVER_SOCKET_FILE_NAME);
   441	}

   443	function codedError(code, message) {
   444	  const error = new Error(message);
   445	  error.code = code;
   446	  return error;
   447	}
   448	
   449	function assertSunPathLimit(socketPath) {
   450	  if (socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) return;
   451	  const byteLength = Buffer.byteLength(socketPath);
   452	  // sun_path is a 104-byte field on macOS INCLUDING the NUL terminator, so the longest path that
   453	  // actually binds is 103 bytes. Reject at 104 to avoid an EINVAL that would otherwise pass this guard.
   454	  if (byteLength <= 103) return;
   455	  throw codedError(
   456	    'ESUNPATHTOOLONG',
   457	    `hf-model-server socket path exceeds the conservative 104-byte sun_path limit (${byteLength} bytes): ${socketPath}. Set SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR to a shorter directory.`,
   458	  );
   459	}
   460

   467	  try {
   468	    dirStat = statApi.lstatSync(socketDir);
   469	  } catch (error) {
   470	    if (error.code !== 'ENOENT') throw error;

   615	function acquireRespawnLockFileAt(lockPath, label = 'respawn', options = {}) {
   616	  const logger = options.log || defaultLog;
   617	  const rel = options.rel || ((p) => p);
   618	  const fsApi = { ...fs, ...(options.fs || {}) };
   619	  const currentLockPath = path.join(ensureCanonicalDir(path.dirname(lockPath)), path.basename(lockPath));
   620	  const tryOpen = () => {
   621	    let fd = null;
   622	    try {
   623	      fd = fsApi.openSync(currentLockPath, 'wx', 0o600);
   624	      fsApi.writeFileSync(fd, `${JSON.stringify({ pid: process.pid, startedAt: new Date().toISOString() }, null, 2)}\n`, 'utf8');
   625	      fsApi.fsyncSync(fd);
   626	      return { acquired: true, fd, path: currentLockPath };
   627	    } catch (error) {
   628	      if (typeof fd === 'number') {
   629	        try {
   630	          fsApi.closeSync(fd);
   631	        } catch {
   632	          // Close failure is secondary to the durable-write failure.
   633	        }
   634	        cleanupTmpFile(currentLockPath, fsApi);
   635	      }
   636	      if (isDurableWriteUnavailable(error)) {
   637	        logDurableWriteUnavailableOnce(logger, `${label} lock acquire`, currentLockPath, error, rel);
   638	        return { acquired: false, path: currentLockPath, reason: 'durable-write-unavailable' };
   639	      }
   640	      throw error;
   641	    }
   642	  };
   643	  try {
   644	    const opened = tryOpen();
   645	    if (!opened.acquired) return opened;
   646	    return opened;
   647	  } catch (error) {
   648	    if (error.code !== 'EEXIST') throw error;
   649	  }
   650	  let raw = '';
   651	  try {
   652	    raw = fsApi.readFileSync(currentLockPath, 'utf8');
   653	  } catch (readErr) {
   654	    if (readErr.code !== 'ENOENT') throw readErr;
   655	  }
   656	  if (raw === '' || isRespawnLockStale(raw, options)) {
   657	    try {
   658	      fsApi.unlinkSync(currentLockPath);
   659	    } catch (unlinkErr) {
   660	      if (unlinkErr.code !== 'ENOENT') throw unlinkErr;
   661	    }
   662	    try {
   663	      const reclaimed = tryOpen();
   664	      if (!reclaimed.acquired) return reclaimed;
   665	      logger(`reclaimed stale ${label} lock ${rel(currentLockPath)}`);
   666	      return reclaimed;
   667	    } catch (retryErr) {
   668	      if (retryErr.code !== 'EEXIST') throw retryErr;
   669	    }
   670	  }
   671	  return { acquired: false, path: currentLockPath };
   672	}

  1473	async function fatalShutdown(reason: string, exitCode: number): Promise<void> {
  1474	  if (shuttingDown) return;
  1475	  shuttingDown = true;
  1476	  console.error(`[context-server] ${reason}`);
  1477	
  1478	  runCleanupStep('sessionManager', () => sessionManager.shutdown());
  1479	  runCleanupStep('retryManager', () => retryManager.stopBackgroundJob());
  1480	  runCleanupStep('shadowEvaluationRuntime', () => shadowEvaluationRuntime.stopShadowEvaluationScheduler());
  1481	  runCleanupStep('scheduledGraphRefresh', () => cancelScheduledRefresh());
  1482	  runCleanupStep('accessTracker', () => accessTracker.reset());
  1483	  runCleanupStep('toolCache', () => toolCache.shutdown());
  1484	  runCleanupStep('launcherIdleMonitor', () => {
  1485	    if (launcherIdleMonitor) {
  1486	      launcherIdleMonitor.stop();
  1487	      launcherIdleMonitor = null;
  1488	    }
  1489	  });
  1490	
  1491	  let deadlineTimer: NodeJS.Timeout | undefined;
  1492	  const cleanup = (async () => {
  1493	    // Drain the file watcher FIRST: fileWatcher.close() awaits in-flight reindex/remove tasks,
  1494	    // which write through requireDb()/getDb() — and getDb() REOPENS the connection if it was
  1495	    // already closed. So closeDb() must run AFTER the drain, otherwise a draining task reopens
  1496	    // the DB and writes fresh WAL frames after the TRUNCATE checkpoint, leaving a non-empty WAL
  1497	    // at rest and defeating the durability guarantee. closeDb() is
  1498	    // synchronous, so it still runs to completion uninterrupted once reached; the SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE
  1499	    // race only bounds the awaited drains before it, and 010's autocheckpoint keeps the at-rest WAL
  1500	    // small even if a pathologically slow drain starves this step.
  1501	    await runAsyncCleanupStep('fileWatcher', async () => {
  1502	      if (fileWatcher) {
  1503	        await fileWatcher.close();
  1504	        fileWatcher = null;
  1505	      }
  1506	    });
  1507	    runCleanupStep('vectorIndex', () => vectorIndex.closeDb());
  1508	    // Close MCP transport on shutdown
  1509	    runCleanupStep('transport', () => {
  1510	      if (transport) {
  1511	        transport.close();
  1512	        transport = null;
  1513	        transportConnectedAt = null;
  1514	      }
  1515	    });
  1516	    await runAsyncCleanupStep('ipcBridge', async () => {
  1517	      if (ipcBridge) {
  1518	        await ipcBridge.close();
  1519	        ipcBridge = null;
  1520	      }
  1521	    });
  1522	    await runAsyncCleanupStep('shutdownHooks', async () => {
  1523	      await runShutdownHooks();
  1524	    });
  1525	    runCleanupStep('registeredTimers', () => clearAllTimers());
  1526	  })();
  1527	
  1528	  const timedOut = await Promise.race([
  1529	    cleanup.then(() => false),
  1530	    new Promise<boolean>((resolve) => {
  1531	      deadlineTimer = registerTimeout(() => resolve(true), SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_MS, { unref: true });
  1532	    }),
  1533	  ]);
  1534	
  1535	  if (deadlineTimer) {
  1536	    clearRegisteredTimer(deadlineTimer);
  1537	  }
  1538	
  1539	  if (timedOut) {
  1540	    console.error(`[context-server] Shutdown deadline exceeded after ${SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_MS}ms`);
  1541	  }
  1542	
  1543	  process.exit(exitCode);
  1544	}

   123	  const socketPath = options.socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')
   124	    ? options.socketPath
   125	    : path.resolve(options.socketPath);
   126	  const log = options.log ?? ((message: string) => console.error(message));
   127	  const maxClients = options.maxClients ?? parseMaxClients();
   128	  const onActivity = options.onActivity ?? (() => undefined);
   129	  if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) {
   130	    fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(socketPath), { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
   131	  }
   132	
   133	  const server = net.createServer((socket) => {
   134	    if (activeSockets.size >= maxClients) {
   135	      log(`[ipc-bridge] refusing secondary connection: max clients ${maxClients} reached`);
   136	      socket.end();
   137	      socket.destroy();
   138	      return;
   139	    }
   140	
   141	    activeSockets.add(socket);
   142	    onActivity();

   145	    const originalWrite = socket.write.bind(socket);

   149	      return originalWrite(chunk as string | Uint8Array, ...(args as [BufferEncoding?, (() => void)?]));
   150	    }) as typeof socket.write;
   151	
   152	    socket.on('data', (chunk) => {
   153	      onActivity();
   154	      totalSecondaryMessagesIn += countJsonRpcFrames(chunk);
   155	    });

   159	    const secondaryServer = options.createServer();
   160	    secondaryServer.connect(transport).catch((error: unknown) => {
   161	      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   162	      log(`[ipc-bridge] secondary connect error: ${message}`);
   163	      socket.destroy();
   164	    });
   165	
   166	    socket.once('close', () => {
   167	      activeSockets.delete(socket);
   168	      activeTransports.delete(socket);
   169	      void transport.close();
   170	      log('[ipc-bridge] disconnect');
   171	    });
   172	    socket.once('error', (error) => {
   173	      log(`[ipc-bridge] socket error: ${error.message}`);
   174	    });
   175	  });
   176	
   177	  try {
   178	    await listenOnce(server, socketPath);
   179	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   180	    const err = error as NodeJS.ErrnoException;
   181	    if (err.code === 'EADDRINUSE' && socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) {
   182	      const listened = await retryTcpListenAfterEaddrInUse(server, socketPath, log);
   183	      if (!listened) {
   184	        log(`[ipc-bridge] tcp socket remained in use at ${socketPath}; secondary bridge disabled`);
   185	        return {
   186	          socketPath,
   187	          close: async () => {
   188	            for (const socket of activeSockets) {
   189	              socket.destroy();
   190	            }
   191	            activeSockets.clear();
   192	            activeTransports.clear();
   193	          },
   194	        };
   195	      }
   196	    } else if (err.code !== 'EADDRINUSE') {
   197	      throw err;
   198	    } else {
   199	      try {
   200	        fs.unlinkSync(socketPath);
   201	      } catch (unlinkError: unknown) {
   202	        const unlinkErr = unlinkError as NodeJS.ErrnoException;
   203	        if (unlinkErr.code !== 'ENOENT') {
   204	          throw unlinkErr;
   205	        }
   206	      }
   207	      await listenOnce(server, socketPath);
   208	    }
   209	  }
   210	
   211	  const address = server.address();
   212	  const listenedPath = typeof address === 'object' && address
   213	    ? `tcp://${address.address}:${address.port}`
   214	    : socketPath;
   215	  if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) {
   216	    fs.chmodSync(socketPath, 0o600);
   217	  }
   218	  activeServer = server;
   219	  activeSocketPath = listenedPath;
   220	  log(`[ipc-bridge] socket listening at ${listenedPath}`);
   221	
   222	  return {
   223	    socketPath: listenedPath,
   224	    close: async () => {
   225	      for (const socket of activeSockets) {
   226	        socket.destroy();
   227	      }
   228	      activeSockets.clear();
   229	      activeTransports.clear();
   230	      await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
   231	        server.close(() => resolve());
   232	      });
   233	      if (activeServer === server) {
   234	        activeServer = null;
   235	        activeSocketPath = null;
   236	      }
   237	      if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) {
   238	        try {
   239	          fs.unlinkSync(socketPath);
   240	        } catch (error: unknown) {
   241	          const err = error as NodeJS.ErrnoException;
   242	          if (err.code !== 'ENOENT') {
   243	            throw err;
   244	          }
   245	        }

    14	
    15	function parsePositiveInteger(value, fallback) {
    16	  if (value === undefined || value === null || String(value).trim() === '') return fallback;
    17	  const parsed = Number.parseInt(String(value).trim(), 10);
    18	  return Number.isFinite(parsed) && parsed > 0 ? parsed : fallback;
    19	}
    20	
    21	function clampProbeTimeoutMs(value) {
    22	  return Math.min(value, MAX_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS);
    23	}
    24	
    25	function resolveProbeTimeoutMs(options = {}) {
    26	  if (Number.isFinite(options.timeoutMs) && options.timeoutMs > 0) {
    27	    return clampProbeTimeoutMs(options.timeoutMs);
    28	  }
    29	  return clampProbeTimeoutMs(parsePositiveInteger(
    30	    (options.env ?? process.env).SPECKIT_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS,
    31	    DEFAULT_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS,
    32	  ));
    33	}

    35	function repoRoot() {
    36	  return path.resolve(__dirname, '..', '..', '..');
    37	}
    38	
    39	function defaultDbDirForService(serviceName) {
    40	  const root = repoRoot();
    41	  if (serviceName === 'mk-spec-memory') {
    42	    return path.join(root, '.opencode', 'skills', 'system-spec-kit', 'mcp_server', 'database');
    43	  }
    44	  if (serviceName === 'mk-code-index') {
    45	    return process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR
    46	      ? path.resolve(process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR)
    47	      : path.join(root, '.opencode', '.spec-kit', 'code-graph', 'database');
    48	  }
    49	  if (serviceName === 'mk-skill-advisor') {
    50	    const advisorOverride = process.env.MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR ?? process.env.SYSTEM_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR;
    51	    return advisorOverride
    52	      ? path.resolve(advisorOverride)
    53	      : path.join(root, '.opencode', 'skills', 'system-skill-advisor', 'mcp_server', 'database');
    54	  }
    55	  throw new Error(`Unknown MCP service name: ${serviceName}`);
    56	}
    57	
    58	function getIpcSocketPath(serviceName, options = {}) {
    59	  if (process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR?.startsWith('tcp://')) {
    60	    return process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR;
    61	  }
    62	  const socketDir = process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR
    63	    ? path.resolve(process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR)
    64	    : path.resolve(options.dbDir ?? defaultDbDirForService(serviceName));
    65	  return path.join(socketDir, SOCKET_FILE_NAME);
    66	}
    67	
    68	function toConnectionOptions(socketPath) {
    69	  if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) {
    70	    return socketPath;
    71	  }
    72	  const url = new URL(socketPath);
    73	  return {
    74	    host: url.hostname,
    75	    port: Number.parseInt(url.port, 10),
    76	  };
    77	}
    78	
    79	function bridgeStdioToSocket(socketPath, options = {}) {
    80	  const input = options.stdin ?? process.stdin;
    81	  const output = options.stdout ?? process.stdout;
    82	  const exit = options.exit ?? ((code) => process.exit(code));
    83	  const socket = net.createConnection(toConnectionOptions(socketPath));
    84	  let closed = false;
    85	
    86	  const closeOnce = (code = 0) => {
    87	    if (closed) return;
    88	    closed = true;
    89	    input.unpipe(socket);
    90	    socket.unpipe(output);
    91	    socket.destroy();
    92	    exit(code);
    93	  };
    94	
    95	  socket.once('connect', () => {
    96	    options.onConnect?.(socket);
    97	    input.pipe(socket);
    98	    socket.pipe(output);
    99	  });
   100	
   101	  socket.once('error', (error) => {
   102	    options.onError?.(error);
   103	    closeOnce(0);
   104	  });
   105	
   106	  socket.once('close', () => {
   107	    closeOnce(0);
   108	  });
   109	
   110	  input.once('end', () => {
   111	    socket.end();
   112	  });
   113	  input.once('close', () => {
   114	    closeOnce(0);
   115	  });
   116	  output.once('error', () => {
   117	    closeOnce(0);
   118	  });
   119	
   120	  return socket;
   121	}
   122	
   123	function probeDaemon(socketPath, options = {}) {
   124	  const timeoutMs = resolveProbeTimeoutMs(options);
   125	  const connect = options.connect ?? ((connectionOptions) => net.createConnection(connectionOptions));
   126	  // deepProbe=true REQUIRES a JSON-RPC initialize reply to call the daemon alive (detects a hung daemon
   127	  // that accepts the connection but never responds). Without it, a bare socket 'connect' counts as alive
   128	  // (connect-ok) — only safe for callers that genuinely need connection-liveness, NOT for the reap/bridge
   129	  // decision (which always passes deepProbe:true). See maybeBridgeLeaseHolder.
   130	  const deepProbe = options.deepProbe === true;
   131	  const id = nextProbeId++;
   132	  const request = `${JSON.stringify({
   133	    jsonrpc: '2.0',
   134	    id,
   135	    method: 'initialize',
   136	    params: {
   137	      protocolVersion: JSON_RPC_PROTOCOL_VERSION,
   138	      capabilities: {},
   139	      clientInfo: { name: 'liveness-probe', version: '0' },
   140	    },
   141	  })}\n`;
   142	
   143	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
   144	    let socket;
   145	    let settled = false;
   146	    let buffer = '';
   147	    let timer;
   148	
   149	    const finish = (status, reason) => {
   150	      if (settled) return;
   151	      settled = true;
   152	      if (timer) clearTimeout(timer);
   153	      if (socket) socket.destroy();
   154	      resolve({ status, reason });
   155	    };
   156	
   157	    try {
   158	      socket = connect(toConnectionOptions(socketPath));
   159	    } catch (error) {
   160	      finish('dead', error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'connect-threw');
   161	      return;
   162	    }
   163	
   164	    timer = setTimeout(() => finish('dead', 'timeout'), timeoutMs);
   165	    timer.unref?.();
   166	
   167	    socket.once('connect', () => {
   168	      if (!deepProbe) {
   169	        finish('alive', 'connect-ok');
   170	        return;
   171	      }
   172	      socket.write(request);
   173	    });
   174	    socket.on('data', (chunk) => {
   175	      buffer += Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk.toString('utf8') : String(chunk ?? '');
   176	      let newlineIndex = buffer.indexOf('\n');
   177	      while (newlineIndex !== -1) {
   178	        const line = buffer.slice(0, newlineIndex).trim();
   179	        buffer = buffer.slice(newlineIndex + 1);
   180	        newlineIndex = buffer.indexOf('\n');
   181	        if (!line) continue;
   182	        try {
   183	          const parsed = JSON.parse(line);
   184	          if (
   185	            parsed &&
   186	            parsed.jsonrpc === '2.0' &&
   187	            parsed.id === id &&
   188	            (Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(parsed, 'result') ||
   189	              Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(parsed, 'error'))
   190	          ) {
   191	            finish('alive', 'json-rpc-reply');
   192	            return;
   193	          }
   194	        } catch {
   195	          // Ignore malformed or unrelated frames; the bounded probe decides liveness.
   196	        }
   197	      }
   198	    });
   199	    socket.once('error', (error) => {
   200	      finish('dead', error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'socket-error');
   201	    });
   202	    socket.once('close', () => {
   203	      finish('dead', 'closed-before-reply');
   204	    });
   205	  });
   206	}

   209	  const raw = buffer.toString('utf8');
   210	  const headerEnd = raw.indexOf('\r\n\r\n');

    25	# Implementation Summary: Single-writer / durability cluster (implemented + tested)

    39	| **Level** | 1 |
    40

    42	---
    43	
    44	<!-- ANCHOR:what-built -->
    45	## What Was Built
    46	
    47	**Implemented + tested (2026-05-30).** All five families of the single-writer/durability cluster landed as one coordinated change set, each finding re-validated at HEAD by symbol and covered by a deterministic regression test:
    48	
    49	- **Family 1+2 — `model-server-supervision.cjs`**: respawn-lock staleness now bounded by listener liveness (DR-005); the lazy demand listener re-arms on a failed spawn (DR-006); idle eviction reaps the root process, not just descendants (DR-012).
    50	- **Family 4 durability — `vector-index-store.ts`**: the clean-shutdown marker is deleted only after a confirmed-successful close (DR-011).
    51	- **Family 4 durability — `reindex.ts`**: same-dimension reindex writes a staging shard and atomic-swaps on success; the worker re-reads the cancel flag mid-run (DR-020 + DR-001-P1-002).
    52	- **Family 4 perimeter — `hf-model-server.cjs`**: the direct-startup unlink path applies the ownership/live-resident perimeter guard, and tcp targets enforce loopback/auth (DR-001-P2-001 + DR-002-P1-001).
    53	- **Family 3 leases — `daemon-detect.ts`**: a stale-looking launcher lease with a live recorded `childPid` is no longer treated as reclaimable (DR-016). OR-R-01 was re-validated as already O_EXCL-remediated at HEAD — no change.
    54	
    55	Verification: mcp + shared + scripts builds exit 0; `node --check` on both `.cjs`; 4 new deterministic suites (26 tests) + 3 regression suites (29 tests) + the leases suite (9 tests) all pass. The original plan's design content (four-family shared-resource map, Harness A/B, apply ordering with precondition gate) drove this implementation and lives in `plan.md`.
    56	
    57	Re-validation already corrected two first-pass items: **OR-R-01 is already O_EXCL-remediated** at HEAD (re-validate only), and **DR-012's mechanism is root-EXCLUSION** in `reapProcessTreeGroups` (`pid !== childPid`), not a missing reap call. The other findings (DR-005/006/016/011/020/001-P2-001 + the folded DR-001-P1-002/DR-002-P1-001) are confirmed-open.
    58	
    59	### Files Changed
    60	
    61	| File | Action | Purpose |
    62	|------|--------|---------|
    63	| `009-…/spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md` | Created | The coordinated design, concurrency-test design, apply ordering, per-family task list |
    64	
    65	(No source files changed in this packet — that is the implementation phase, deliberately not run yet.)
    66	
    67	<!-- /ANCHOR:what-built -->
    68	---
    69	
    70	<!-- ANCHOR:how-delivered -->
    71	## How It Was Delivered
    72	
    73	A multi-agent design pass produced the coordinated plan; it was then re-validated against HEAD-read source (correcting the OR-R-01 already-done and DR-012 mechanism items) and cross-checked against `031/review/review-report.md`. The plan was captured as this committed packet so the work is durable and ready to execute as one careful effort — exactly as the handover mandates (one coordinated packet, after the launcher-WIP precondition gate, with a deterministic two-launcher concurrency test). Implementation was intentionally NOT attempted at the tail of a long session because this is the highest-blast-radius surface in the repo (the program exists because of a DB-corruption incident here) and the design itself requires per-finding HEAD re-validation plus a gate.
    74	
    75	<!-- /ANCHOR:how-delivered -->
    76	---
    77	
    78	<!-- ANCHOR:decisions -->
    79	## Key Decisions
    80	
    81	| Decision | Why |
    82	|----------|-----|
    83	| Design first as a committed packet, then implement | The ready plan was captured durably so the implementation could be re-validated against it per family rather than rushed |
    84	| Implement as one coordinated change set per family | The findings share a respawn lock / root-liveness authority / leases / marker; piecemeal fixes re-collide (handover mandate) |
    85	| Honor the precondition gate before Family-3 | The code-graph launcher is adjacent-session territory; OR-R-01 is already remediated |
    86	| Deterministic Harness A/B, no real sleeps | Concurrency bugs need reproducible RED-before / GREEN-after evidence via injected time/liveness/spawn |
    87	
    88	<!-- /ANCHOR:decisions -->

   107	
   108	<!-- ANCHOR:limitations -->
   109	## Known Limitations
   110	
   111	1. **Concurrency tests are deterministic, not live.** Harness A/B inject `nowMs`/`liveness`/`spawnFn`/`signal` to force races without real sleeps. A live two-launcher integration run (real processes) remains gated on a working `onnxruntime` tree, as documented in packet 005 — these unit-level suites assert the corrected logic, not end-to-end residency.
   112	2. **Family-3 scope.** DR-016 was fixed in `daemon-detect.ts` (this skill's territory); OR-R-01 was only re-validated (already O_EXCL in the code-graph launcher, adjacent-session territory — untouched).
   113	3. **`reindex.ts` cluster fix sits alongside the C2 cancel edit** (packet 008); both were re-validated together so the staging-swap and cancel-re-read are consistent.
   114	
   115	<!-- /ANCHOR:limitations -->
