     1	import { createRequire } from 'node:module';
     2	
     3	import { afterEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest';
     4	
     5	type ProcessRowsRunner = () => string | Array<Record<string, number>>;
     6	type SignalFn = (pid: number, sig: number | string) => boolean;
     7	
     8	const require = createRequire(import.meta.url);
     9	const launcher = require('../../../../bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs') as {
    10	  buildLeaseObject: (childPid?: number | null, startedAt?: string) => Record<string, unknown>;
    11	  createCrashLoopGuard: (options?: Record<string, unknown>) => { recordDeath: () => Record<string, unknown> };
    12	  getWatchdogConfig: (env?: Record<string, string | undefined>, warn?: (message: string) => void) => Record<string, unknown>;
    13	  normalizeWatchdogGraceMs: (rawGraceMs: unknown, warn?: (message: string) => void) => number;
    14	  reapProcessTreeGroups: (
    15	    childPid: number,
    16	    options?: { runner?: ProcessRowsRunner; signal?: SignalFn; snapshotPids?: number[] },
    17	  ) => void;
    18	  isRespawnLockStale: (raw: string, options?: { liveness?: (pid: number) => string; nowMs?: number; staleMs?: number }) => boolean;
    19	  refreshDescendantSnapshot: (childPid: number, runner?: ProcessRowsRunner) => number[];
    20	  sampleProcessTreeRssMb: (childPid: number, runner?: ProcessRowsRunner) => number | null;
    21	  shouldSkipLaunch: (child: { exitCode: number | null; signalCode: string | null } | null) => boolean;
    22	  signalProcess: (pid: number, sig: number | string) => boolean;
    23	  superviseChildExit: (
    24	    event: Record<string, unknown>,
    25	    actions: Record<string, unknown>,
    26	  ) => Record<string, unknown>;
    27	};

    52	  it('backs off and relaunches after a single unexpected child death', () => {

    73	    const actions = {
    74	      crashLoopGuard: guard,
    75	      clearLease: () => calls.push('clearLease'),
    76	      reapProcessGroup: (pid: number) => calls.push(`reap:${pid}`),
    77	      mirrorSignal: () => calls.push('mirrorSignal'),
    78	      exit: (code: number) => calls.push(`exit:${code}`),
    79	      scheduleRelaunch: (backoffMs: number) => calls.push(`relaunch:${backoffMs}`),
    80	    };
    81	
    82	    launcher.superviseChildExit({ code: 1, signal: null, childPid: 321, intentional: false }, actions);
    83	    const result = launcher.superviseChildExit({ code: 1, signal: null, childPid: 321, intentional: false }, actions);
    84	
    85	    expect(result).toMatchObject({ action: 'give-up', deathsInWindow: 2 });
    86	    expect(calls).toEqual(['relaunch:25', 'clearLease', 'reap:321', 'exit:1']);
    87	  });
    88	
    89	  it('clamps configured RSS self-exit grace to exceed the daemon shutdown deadline', () => {
    90	    const warnings: string[] = [];
    91	    const graceMs = launcher.normalizeWatchdogGraceMs('5000', (message) => warnings.push(message));
    92	    const config = launcher.getWatchdogConfig(
    93	      {
    94	        SPECKIT_CONTEXT_SERVER_MAX_RSS_MB: '1024',
    95	        SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_RSS_SELF_EXIT: '1',

    97	      },
    98	      (message) => warnings.push(message),
    99	    );
   100	
   101	    expect(graceMs).toBeGreaterThan(5000);
   102	    expect(config).toMatchObject({ enabled: true, graceMs });
   103	    expect(warnings.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
   104	  });
   105

   109	      maxRssMb: 1024,
   110	    });
   111	  });
   112	
   113	  // The give-up reap must still kill an orphaned sidecar after a HARD daemon death, when the
   114	  // detached sidecar has re-parented to pid 1 and the dead childPid is gone from `ps`. This is the case
   115	  // a fresh-walk-anchored-on-childPid reap silently no-ops on; the before-death snapshot closes it.
   116	  it('reaps a re-parented orphan sidecar from the before-death snapshot when the dead child is gone from ps (REQ-007)', () => {
   117	    vi.useFakeTimers();
   118	    try {
   119	      const signals: Array<[number, number | string]> = [];
   120	      const alive = new Set([101]);
   121	      launcher.reapProcessTreeGroups(100, {
   122	        // childPid 100 is DEAD (absent); sidecar 101 re-parented to pid 1; 200 is unrelated.
   123	        runner: () => `
   124	          1 0 100
   125	          101 1 2048
   126	          200 1 8192
   127	        `,
   128	        signal: (pid, sig) => {
   129	          if (sig === 0) return alive.has(pid);
   130	          signals.push([pid, sig]);
   131	          return true;
   132	        },
   133	        snapshotPids: [101],
   134	      });
   135	      expect(signals).toEqual([[-101, 'SIGTERM'], [101, 'SIGTERM']]);
   136	      vi.advanceTimersByTime(1000);
   137	      expect(signals).toEqual([[-101, 'SIGTERM'], [101, 'SIGTERM'], [-101, 'SIGKILL'], [101, 'SIGKILL']]);
   138	    } finally {
   139	      vi.useRealTimers();
   140	    }
   141	  });
   142

   155	      },
   156	      snapshotPids: [],
   157	    });
   158	    expect(signals).toEqual([[-101, 'SIGTERM'], [101, 'SIGTERM']]);
   159	  });

   210

   213	    });
   214	    expect(launcher.signalProcess(4242, 'SIGTERM')).toBe(false);
   215

   217	      throw Object.assign(new Error('no such process'), { code: 'ESRCH' });
   218	    });
   219	    expect(launcher.signalProcess(4242, 'SIGKILL')).toBe(false);
   220	
   221	    spy.mockImplementation(() => {
   222	      throw Object.assign(new Error('boom'), { code: 'EINVAL' });
   223	    });
   224	    expect(() => launcher.signalProcess(4242, 'SIGKILL')).toThrow('boom');

   227	  // Regression: the F3 duplicate-spawn guard must NOT permanently disable F1's crash-loop relaunch.
   228	  // A one-shot "ever launched" flag blocked scheduleRelaunch->launchServer after a child exited;
   229	  // the correct guard blocks only while a child is CURRENTLY running.
   230	  it('shouldSkipLaunch blocks only a currently-running child, allowing relaunch after exit (REQ-004 / F1 relaunch)', () => {
   231	    expect(launcher.shouldSkipLaunch(null)).toBe(false); // first launch allowed
   232	    expect(launcher.shouldSkipLaunch({ exitCode: null, signalCode: null })).toBe(true); // running -> skip duplicate
   233	    expect(launcher.shouldSkipLaunch({ exitCode: 1, signalCode: null })).toBe(false); // exited -> relaunch ALLOWED (the fix)
   234	    expect(launcher.shouldSkipLaunch({ exitCode: null, signalCode: 'SIGKILL' })).toBe(false); // killed -> relaunch allowed
   235	  });

   237	  it('isRespawnLockStale reclaims dead/unparseable/aged locks but keeps a live fresh one (REQ-003 stale-lock reclaim)', () => {
   238	    const liveness = (pid: number) => (pid === 111 ? 'alive' : 'dead');
   239	    const now = Date.parse('2026-05-28T00:01:00.000Z');

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	import { spawn, type ChildProcess, type ChildProcessWithoutNullStreams } from 'node:child_process';
     2	import { copyFileSync, existsSync, mkdirSync, mkdtempSync, readFileSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
     3	import { tmpdir } from 'node:os';
     4	import { dirname, join, resolve } from 'node:path';
     5	import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
     6	import { afterEach, describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
     7	
     8	// These paths are test fixtures coupled to the production launcher layout.
     9	// If a launcher moves, update the fixture path and copied production file together.
    10	
    11	interface LauncherRun {
    12	  child: ChildProcessWithoutNullStreams;
    13	  stdout: string;
    14	  stderr: string;
    15	}
    16	
    17	const repoRoot = resolve(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '../../../../..');
    18	const launcherRelativePath = '.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs';
    19	const pidFileRelativePath = '.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.mk-spec-memory-launcher.json';
    20	const tempDirs: string[] = [];
    21	const launcherRuns: LauncherRun[] = [];
    22	
    23	function createWorkspace(options: { ignoreChildSigterm?: boolean } = {}): { root: string; launcherPath: string; pidFilePath: string } {
    24	  const root = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'mk-spec-memory-lease-'));
    25	  tempDirs.push(root);
    26	
    27	  const launcherPath = join(root, launcherRelativePath);
    28	  mkdirSync(dirname(launcherPath), { recursive: true });
    29	  copyFileSync(join(repoRoot, launcherRelativePath), launcherPath);
    30	
    31	  const contextServer = join(root, '.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js');
    32	  const graphBackfill = join(root, '.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.js');
    33	  const descriptionGenerator = join(root, '.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/spec-folder/generate-description.js');
    34	  mkdirSync(dirname(contextServer), { recursive: true });
    35	  mkdirSync(dirname(graphBackfill), { recursive: true });
    36	  mkdirSync(dirname(descriptionGenerator), { recursive: true });
    37	  writeFileSync(
    38	    contextServer,
    39	    options.ignoreChildSigterm
    40	      ? "process.on('SIGTERM', () => {}); process.on('SIGINT', () => process.exit(0)); setInterval(() => {}, 1000);\n"
    41	      : "process.on('SIGTERM', () => process.exit(0)); process.on('SIGINT', () => process.exit(0)); setInterval(() => {}, 1000);\n",
    42	    'utf8',
    43	  );
    44	  writeFileSync(graphBackfill, 'export {};\n', 'utf8');
    45	  writeFileSync(descriptionGenerator, 'export {};\n', 'utf8');
    46	
    47	  return { root, launcherPath, pidFilePath: join(root, pidFileRelativePath) };
    48	}
    49	
    50	function spawnLauncher(launcherPath: string, root: string, env: NodeJS.ProcessEnv = {}): LauncherRun {
    51	  const baseEnv = { ...process.env };
    52	  delete baseEnv.MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_STRICT_SINGLE_WRITER;
    53	  delete baseEnv.MK_CODE_INDEX_STRICT_SINGLE_WRITER;
    54	  delete baseEnv.MK_SPEC_MEMORY_STRICT_SINGLE_WRITER;
    55	
    56	  const run: LauncherRun = {
    57	    child: spawn(process.execPath, [launcherPath], {
    58	      cwd: root,
    59	      env: { ...baseEnv, ...env },
    60	      stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
    61	    }),
    62	    stdout: '',
    63	    stderr: '',
    64	  };
    65	  run.child.stdout.setEncoding('utf8');
    66	  run.child.stderr.setEncoding('utf8');
    67	  run.child.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => {
    68	    run.stdout += chunk;
    69	  });
    70	  run.child.stderr.on('data', (chunk) => {
    71	    run.stderr += chunk;
    72	  });
    73	  launcherRuns.push(run);
    74	  return run;
    75	}
    76	
    77	async function waitFor(predicate: () => boolean, timeoutMs: number, label: string): Promise<void> {
    78	  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
    79	  while (Date.now() <= deadline) {
    80	    if (predicate()) return;
    81	    await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 25));
    82	  }
    83	  throw new Error(`Timed out waiting for ${label}`);
    84	}
    85	
    86	async function waitForExit(
    87	  child: ChildProcess,
    88	  timeoutMs: number,
    89	): Promise<{ code: number | null; signal: NodeJS.Signals | null }> {
    90	  if (child.exitCode !== null || child.signalCode !== null) {
    91	    return { code: child.exitCode, signal: child.signalCode };
    92	  }
    93	  return await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    94	    const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
    95	      child.off('exit', onExit);
    96	      reject(new Error(`Timed out waiting for pid ${child.pid} to exit`));
    97	    }, timeoutMs);
    98	    const onExit = (code: number | null, signal: NodeJS.Signals | null) => {
    99	      clearTimeout(timeout);
   100	      resolve({ code, signal });
   101	    };
   102	    child.once('exit', onExit);
   103	  });
   104	}
   105	
   106	async function waitForStdoutClose(run: LauncherRun): Promise<void> {
   107	  if (run.child.stdout.closed) return;
   108	  await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
   109	    run.child.stdout.once('close', () => resolve());
   110	  });
   111	}
   112	
   113	async function terminate(run: LauncherRun): Promise<void> {
   114	  if (run.child.exitCode !== null || run.child.signalCode !== null) return;
   115	  run.child.kill('SIGTERM');
   116	    try {
   117	      await waitForExit(run.child, 1000);
   118	    } catch {
   119	      run.child.kill('SIGKILL');
   120	      try {
   121	        await waitForExit(run.child, 1000);
   122	      } catch {
   123	        // Best-effort teardown; the temp dir cleanup below is authoritative.
   124	      }
   125	    }
   126	  }
   127	
   128	function readLeasePid(pidFilePath: string): number | null {
   129	  try {
   130	    const parsed = JSON.parse(readFileSync(pidFilePath, 'utf8')) as { pid?: unknown };
   131	    return typeof parsed.pid === 'number' ? parsed.pid : null;
   132	  } catch {
   133	    return null;
   134	  }
   135	}
   136	
   137	async function waitForLeasePid(pidFilePath: string, pid: number | undefined): Promise<void> {
   138	  if (typeof pid !== 'number') throw new Error('launcher pid was not assigned');
   139	  await waitFor(() => readLeasePid(pidFilePath) === pid, 2000, `lease pid ${pid}`);
   140	}
   141	
   142	async function createDeadPid(): Promise<number> {
   143	  const child = spawn(process.execPath, ['-e', 'setInterval(() => {}, 1000);'], {
   144	    stdio: 'ignore',
   145	  });
   146	  await new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
   147	    child.once('spawn', () => resolve());
   148	    child.once('error', reject);
   149	  });
   150	  if (typeof child.pid !== 'number') {
   151	    throw new Error('dead-pid child did not get a pid');
   152	  }
   153	  const pid = child.pid;
   154	  child.kill('SIGTERM');
   155	  await waitForExit(child, 1000);
   156	  return pid;
   157	}
   158	
   159	async function createLivePid(): Promise<ChildProcess> {
   160	  const child = spawn(process.execPath, ['-e', 'setInterval(() => {}, 1000);'], {
   161	    stdio: 'ignore',
   162	  });
   163	  await new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
   164	    child.once('spawn', () => resolve());
   165	    child.once('error', reject);
   166	  });
   167	  if (typeof child.pid !== 'number') {
   168	    throw new Error('live-pid child did not get a pid');
   169	  }
   170	  return child;
   171	}

   178	      if (run) await terminate(run);
   179	    }
   180	    while (tempDirs.length > 0) {
   181	      const dir = tempDirs.pop();
   182	      if (dir) rmSync(dir, { recursive: true, force: true });
   183	    }
   184	  });

   325	    expect(existsSync(workspace.pidFilePath)).toBe(true);
   326	  });
   327	});

codex
The launcher path is sharper than the prior hypothesis: healthy live leases bridge; dead or wedged IPC triggers respawn. The risk is the respawn path waits for process death, not for the DB clean-close invariant, so I’m checking graph-churn separately to avoid overstating a shared cause.

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts | sed -n '1066,1145p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
  1160	 *
  1161	 * Used to make `last_save_at` idempotent: a re-derive that produced no content change
  1162	 * must not rewrite the file (otherwise every save churns hundreds of packets' timestamps
  1163	 * and buries real diffs in the working tree). Key order is normalized so a payload loaded
  1164	 * from disk compares equal to a freshly-constructed one.
  1165	 *
  1166	 * @param a - First metadata payload
  1167	 * @param b - Second metadata payload
  1168	 * @returns True when the two are equal apart from `last_save_at` / `save_lineage`
  1169	 */
  1170	export function graphMetadataEqualIgnoringVolatile(a: GraphMetadata, b: GraphMetadata): boolean {
  1171	  const strip = (metadata: GraphMetadata): unknown => {
  1172	    const clone = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(metadata)) as GraphMetadata;
  1173	    const derived = clone.derived as Record<string, unknown>;
  1174	    delete derived.last_save_at;
  1175	    delete derived.save_lineage;
  1176	    return canonicalizeForCompare(clone);
  1177	  };
  1178	  return JSON.stringify(strip(a)) === JSON.stringify(strip(b));
  1179	}
  1180	
  1181	/**
  1182	 * Serialize graph metadata as stable pretty-printed JSON.
  1183	 *
  1184	 * @param metadata - Graph metadata payload to serialize
  1185	 * @returns JSON document terminated with a trailing newline
  1186	 */
  1187	export function serializeGraphMetadata(metadata: GraphMetadata): string {
  1188	  return `${JSON.stringify(metadata, null, 2)}\n`;
  1189	}
  1190	
  1191	/**
  1192	 * Write graph metadata to disk using an atomic temp-file swap.
  1193	 *
  1194	 * @param filePath - Destination graph-metadata path
  1195	 * @param metadata - Metadata payload to persist
  1196	 */
  1197	export function writeGraphMetadataFile(filePath: string, metadata: GraphMetadata): void {
  1198	  const resolvedFilePath = path.resolve(filePath);
  1199	  const parentDir = path.dirname(resolvedFilePath);
  1200	  let canonicalParentDir: string;
  1201	  try {
  1202	    canonicalParentDir = fs.realpathSync(parentDir);
  1203	  } catch {
  1204	    throw new Error(`Graph metadata parent directory is invalid or contains a broken symlink: ${parentDir}`);
  1205	  }
  1206	
  1207	  const canonicalFilePath = path.join(canonicalParentDir, path.basename(resolvedFilePath));
  1208	  if (!canClassifyAsGraphMetadataPath(canonicalFilePath)) {
  1209	    throw new Error(`Refusing to write graph metadata outside a supported specs root: ${canonicalFilePath}`);
  1210	  }
  1211	
  1212	  const content = serializeGraphMetadata(metadata);
  1213	  // Counter + random suffix avoids same-process temp-path collisions during overlapping writes.
  1214	  const tempPath = `${canonicalFilePath}.tmp-${process.pid}-${tempCounter++}-${crypto.randomBytes(4).toString('hex')}`;
  1215	
  1216	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(canonicalFilePath), { recursive: true });
  1217	  fs.writeFileSync(tempPath, content, 'utf-8');
  1218	  fs.renameSync(tempPath, canonicalFilePath);
  1219	}
  1220	
  1221	/**
  1222	 * Refresh the graph-metadata file for a single spec folder.
  1223	 *
  1224	 * @param specFolderPath - Absolute path to the target spec folder
  1225	 * @param options - Refresh controls such as timestamp and status override
  1226	 * @returns Saved metadata plus the final output path and creation status
  1227	 */
  1228	export function refreshGraphMetadataForSpecFolder(
  1229	  specFolderPath: string,
  1230	  options: GraphMetadataRefreshOptions = {},
  1231	): GraphMetadataRefreshResult {
  1232	  const resolvedSpecFolderPath = path.resolve(specFolderPath);
  1233	  let canonicalSpecFolderPath: string;
  1234	  try {
  1235	    canonicalSpecFolderPath = fs.realpathSync(resolvedSpecFolderPath);
  1236	  } catch {
  1237	    throw new Error(`Spec folder path is invalid or contains a broken symlink: ${resolvedSpecFolderPath}`);
  1238	  }
  1239	
  1240	  if (!canClassifyAsGraphMetadataPath(path.join(canonicalSpecFolderPath, GRAPH_METADATA_FILENAME))) {
  1241	    throw new Error(`Spec folder is not under a supported specs root: ${canonicalSpecFolderPath}`);
  1242	  }
  1243	
  1244	  const filePath = path.join(canonicalSpecFolderPath, GRAPH_METADATA_FILENAME);
  1245	  const existing = loadGraphMetadata(filePath);
  1246	  const refreshed = deriveGraphMetadata(canonicalSpecFolderPath, existing, {
  1247	    ...options,
  1248	    saveLineage: options.saveLineage ?? 'graph_only',
  1249	  });
  1250	  const merged = mergeGraphMetadata(existing, refreshed);
  1251	
  1252	  // Idempotent write: when a re-derive produced no content change (only last_save_at
  1253	  // would move), skip the write entirely. Without this, every save rewrites this
  1254	  // packet's last_save_at, and a broad re-index churns hundreds of files' timestamps
  1255	  // with no real delta — burying genuine changes in working-tree noise. A no-op
  1256	  // re-derive has no new state worth stamping, so returning the existing payload is
  1257	  // correct. Any real change still writes (and still bumps last_save_at).
  1258	  if (existing && graphMetadataEqualIgnoringVolatile(existing, merged)) {
  1259	    return {
  1260	      filePath,
  1261	      metadata: existing,
  1262	      created: false,
  1263	    };
  1264	  }
  1265	
  1266	  writeGraphMetadataFile(filePath, merged);
  1267	
  1268	  return {
  1269	    filePath,
  1270	    metadata: merged,
  1271	    created: existing === null,
  1272	  };
  1273	}
  1274	
  1275	export function refreshGraphMetadata(
  1276	  specFolderPath: string,
  1277	  options: GraphMetadataRefreshOptions = {},
  1278	): GraphMetadataRefreshResult {
  1279	  return refreshGraphMetadataForSpecFolder(specFolderPath, options);
  1280	}

  1066	export function deriveGraphMetadata(
  1067	  specFolderPath: string,
  1068	  existing: GraphMetadata | null = null,
  1069	  options: GraphMetadataRefreshOptions = {},
  1070	): GraphMetadata {
  1071	  const nowIso = toIsoString(options.now);
  1072	  const collectedDocs = collectPacketDocs(specFolderPath);
  1073	  const docs = collectedDocs.docs;
  1074	  const specFolder = resolveSpecFolderPath(specFolderPath);
  1075	  const packetId = specFolder;
  1076	  const triggerPhrases = normalizeUnique(docs.flatMap((doc) => doc.triggerPhrases)).slice(0, GRAPH_METADATA_TRIGGER_PHRASE_LIMIT);
  1077	  const causalSummary = deriveCausalSummary(docs);
  1078	  const keyFiles = deriveKeyFiles(specFolderPath, specFolder, docs);
  1079	  const keyTopics = deriveKeyTopics(docs, triggerPhrases, causalSummary);
  1080	  const sourceDocs = docs.map((doc) => doc.relativePath);
  1081	  const entities = deriveEntities(specFolder, docs, keyFiles);
  1082	
  1083	  const derived: GraphMetadataDerived = {
  1084	    trigger_phrases: triggerPhrases,
  1085	    key_topics: keyTopics,
  1086	    importance_tier: deriveImportanceTier(docs),
  1087	    status: deriveStatus(docs, collectedDocs.availability, options.statusOverride, existing?.derived.status),
  1088	    key_files: keyFiles,
  1089	    entities,
  1090	    causal_summary: causalSummary,
  1091	    created_at: existing?.derived.created_at ?? nowIso,
  1092	    last_save_at: nowIso,
  1093	    save_lineage: options.saveLineage,
  1094	    last_accessed_at: existing?.derived.last_accessed_at ?? null,
  1095	    source_docs: sourceDocs,
  1096	    // Chronology pointer is owned by the canonical save path (generate-context.ts);
  1097	    // a derive/re-derive must carry it forward, never invent or drop it.
  1098	    last_active_child_id: existing?.derived.last_active_child_id ?? null,
  1099	    last_active_at: existing?.derived.last_active_at ?? null,
  1100	  };
  1101	
  1102	  return graphMetadataSchema.parse({
  1103	    schema_version: GRAPH_METADATA_SCHEMA_VERSION,
  1104	    packet_id: packetId,
  1105	    spec_folder: specFolder,
  1106	    parent_id: resolveParentId(specFolder),
  1107	    children_ids: resolveChildrenIds(specFolderPath, specFolder),
  1108	    migrated: existing?.migrated ?? undefined,
  1109	    migration_source: existing?.migration_source ?? undefined,
  1110	    manual: existing?.manual ?? createEmptyGraphMetadataManual(),
  1111	    derived,
  1112	  });
  1113	}
  1114	
  1115	/**
  1116	 * Merge refreshed derived metadata with persisted manual relationships.
  1117	 *
  1118	 * @param existing - Previously saved graph metadata, if present
  1119	 * @param refreshed - Newly derived graph metadata snapshot
  1120	 * @returns Schema-validated merged metadata payload
  1121	 */
  1122	export function mergeGraphMetadata(
  1123	  existing: GraphMetadata | null,
  1124	  refreshed: GraphMetadata,
  1125	): GraphMetadata {
  1126	  return graphMetadataSchema.parse({
  1127	    ...refreshed,
  1128	    migrated: existing?.migrated ?? refreshed.migrated,
  1129	    migration_source: existing?.migration_source ?? refreshed.migration_source,
  1130	    manual: existing?.manual ?? createEmptyGraphMetadataManual(),
  1131	    derived: {
  1132	      ...refreshed.derived,
  1133	      created_at: existing?.derived.created_at ?? refreshed.derived.created_at,
  1134	      last_accessed_at: existing?.derived.last_accessed_at ?? refreshed.derived.last_accessed_at,
  1135	      last_active_child_id: existing?.derived.last_active_child_id ?? refreshed.derived.last_active_child_id ?? null,
  1136	      last_active_at: existing?.derived.last_active_at ?? refreshed.derived.last_active_at ?? null,
  1137	    },
  1138	  });
  1139	}
  1140	
  1141	function canonicalizeForCompare(value: unknown): unknown {
  1142	  if (Array.isArray(value)) {
  1143	    // Array order is content-significant (e.g. entities, children_ids) — preserve it.
  1144	    return value.map(canonicalizeForCompare);
  1145	  }

     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Backfill Graph Metadata
     3	// Usage:
     4	//   node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.js [--dry-run] [--root <specs-dir>]
     5	//   node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.js [--dry-run] --active-only [--root <specs-dir>]
     6	//
     7	// Default behavior is inclusive: all packet folders under the selected specs root
     8	// are refreshed, including z_archive/ and z_future/. Use --active-only only when
     9	// you intentionally want to skip archived trees.
    10	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    11	
    12	import fs from 'node:fs';
    13	import path from 'node:path';
    14	
    15	import {
    16	  deriveGraphMetadata,
    17	  loadGraphMetadata,
    18	  refreshGraphMetadataForSpecFolder,
    19	  type GraphMetadata,
    20	} from '@spec-kit/mcp-server/api';
    21	import { dirnameFromImportMeta, isMainModule } from '../lib/esm-entry.js';
    22	
    23	const moduleDir = dirnameFromImportMeta(import.meta.url);
    24	
    25	const SPEC_FOLDER_RE = /^\d{3}(?:[-_].+)?$/;
    26	const EXCLUDED_DIRS = new Set(['memory', 'scratch', 'node_modules', '.git']);
    27	const ARCHIVE_SEGMENT_RE = /(^|\/)(z_archive|z_future)(\/|$)/;
    28	
    29	interface BackfillSummary {
    30	  dryRun: boolean;
    31	  root: string;
    32	  totalSpecFolders: number;
    33	  created: number;
    34	  refreshed: number;
    35	  existing: number;
    36	  lineageStamped: number;
    37	  reviewFlags: Array<{ specFolder: string; flags: string[] }>;
    38	}
    39	
    40	export interface BackfillOptions {
    41	  dryRun: boolean;
    42	  root: string;
    43	  activeOnly?: boolean;
    44	}
    45	
    46	function resolveRepoRoot(): string {
    47	  const cwdCandidate = path.resolve(process.cwd());
    48	  if (fs.existsSync(path.join(cwdCandidate, '.opencode', 'specs'))) {
    49	    return cwdCandidate;
    50	  }
    51	
    52	  let current = path.resolve(moduleDir);
    53	  let lastMatch: string | null = null;
    54	  while (true) {
    55	    if (fs.existsSync(path.join(current, '.opencode', 'specs'))) {
    56	      lastMatch = current;
    57	    }
    58	    const parent = path.dirname(current);
    59	    if (parent === current) {
    60	      break;
    61	    }
    62	    current = parent;
    63	  }
    64	  if (lastMatch) {
    65	    return lastMatch;
    66	  }
    67	  return path.resolve(moduleDir, '..', '..', '..', '..', '..');
    68	}
    69	
    70	function parseArgs(argv: string[]): { dryRun: boolean; root: string; activeOnly: boolean } {
    71	  let dryRun = false;
    72	  let root = path.join(resolveRepoRoot(), '.opencode', 'specs');
    73	  let activeOnly = false;
    74	
    75	  for (let index = 0; index < argv.length; index += 1) {
    76	    const arg = argv[index];
    77	    if (arg === '--dry-run') {
    78	      dryRun = true;
    79	      continue;
    80	    }
    81	    if (arg === '--active-only') {
    82	      activeOnly = true;
    83	      continue;
    84	    }
    85	    if (arg === '--include-archive') {
    86	      activeOnly = false;
    87	      continue;
    88	    }
    89	    if (arg === '--root') {
    90	      root = path.resolve(argv[index + 1] ?? root);
    91	      index += 1;
    92	    }
    93	  }
    94	
    95	  return { dryRun, root, activeOnly };
    96	}
    97	
    98	function isSpecFolder(dirPath: string): boolean {
    99	  const base = path.basename(dirPath);
   100	  return SPEC_FOLDER_RE.test(base) && fs.existsSync(path.join(dirPath, 'spec.md'));
   101	}
   102	
   103	function isArchivedTraversalPath(dirPath: string): boolean {
   104	  return ARCHIVE_SEGMENT_RE.test(dirPath.replace(/\\/g, '/'));
   105	}
   106	
   107	/**
   108	 * Collect spec folders eligible for graph-metadata refresh.
   109	 *
   110	 * @param root - Specs root to traverse
   111	 * @param options - Traversal controls such as `activeOnly`
   112	 * @returns Sorted list of discovered spec-folder paths
   113	 */
   114	export function collectSpecFolders(
   115	  root: string,
   116	  options: Pick<BackfillOptions, 'activeOnly'> = {},
   117	): string[] {
   118	  const folders: string[] = [];
   119	  const activeOnly = options.activeOnly ?? false;
   120	
   121	  function walk(currentPath: string): void {
   122	    if (activeOnly && isArchivedTraversalPath(currentPath)) {
   123	      return;
   124	    }
   125	
   126	    let entries: fs.Dirent[];
   127	    try {
   128	      entries = fs.readdirSync(currentPath, { withFileTypes: true });
   129	    } catch {
   130	      return;
   131	    }
   132	
   133	    if (isSpecFolder(currentPath)) {
   134	      folders.push(currentPath);
   135	    }
   136	
   137	    for (const entry of entries) {
   138	      if (!entry.isDirectory()) {
   139	        continue;
   140	      }
   141	      if (EXCLUDED_DIRS.has(entry.name) || entry.name.startsWith('.')) {
   142	        continue;
   143	      }
   144	      walk(path.join(currentPath, entry.name));
   145	    }
   146	  }
   147	
   148	  walk(root);
   149	  return folders.sort();
   150	}
   151	
   152	/**
   153	 * Derive human-review flags for a refreshed graph-metadata payload.
   154	 *
   155	 * @param specFolderPath - Absolute path to the packet folder being inspected
   156	 * @param metadata - Derived or refreshed graph metadata for the packet
   157	 * @returns List of review flags describing low-confidence derivations
   158	 */
   159	export function collectReviewFlags(specFolderPath: string, metadata: GraphMetadata): string[] {
   160	  const flags: string[] = [];
   161	  const specDoc = fs.existsSync(path.join(specFolderPath, 'spec.md'))
   162	    ? fs.readFileSync(path.join(specFolderPath, 'spec.md'), 'utf-8')
   163	    : '';
   164	  const planDoc = fs.existsSync(path.join(specFolderPath, 'plan.md'))
   165	    ? fs.readFileSync(path.join(specFolderPath, 'plan.md'), 'utf-8')
   166	    : '';
   167	
   168	  if (metadata.derived.status === 'planned' && !/\nstatus:\s*["']?(planned|complete|in_progress|blocked)/i.test(`${specDoc}\n${planDoc}`)) {
   169	    flags.push('ambiguous_status');
   170	  }
   171	  if (
   172	    metadata.derived.causal_summary === 'Packet metadata derived from canonical spec documents.'
   173	    || metadata.derived.causal_summary.trim().length < 40
   174	  ) {
   175	    flags.push('missing_summary');
   176	  }
   177	  if (/(depends on|supersedes|related to)/i.test(`${specDoc}\n${planDoc}`)) {
   178	    flags.push('prose_relationship_hints');
   179	  }
   180	  if (metadata.derived.source_docs.length < 3) {
   181	    flags.push('thin_source_docs');
   182	  }
   183	
   184	  return flags;
   185	}
   186	
   187	/**
   188	 * Backfill graph-metadata files across the selected specs tree.
   189	 *
   190	 * @param options - Backfill execution options
   191	 * @returns Aggregate summary of created, refreshed, and flagged packets
   192	 */
   193	export function runBackfill({ dryRun, root, activeOnly = false }: BackfillOptions): BackfillSummary {
   194	  const specFolders = collectSpecFolders(root, { activeOnly });
   195	  const summary: BackfillSummary = {
   196	    dryRun,
   197	    root,
   198	    totalSpecFolders: specFolders.length,
   199	    created: 0,
   200	    refreshed: 0,
   201	    existing: 0,
   202	    lineageStamped: 0,
   203	    reviewFlags: [],
   204	  };
   205	
   206	  for (const specFolderPath of specFolders) {
   207	    const graphPath = path.join(specFolderPath, 'graph-metadata.json');
   208	    const existing = loadGraphMetadata(graphPath);
   209	    const saveLineage = existing?.derived.save_lineage ?? 'graph_only';
   210	    if (existing) {
   211	      summary.existing += 1;
   212	    }
   213	
   214	    const metadata = dryRun
   215	      ? deriveGraphMetadata(specFolderPath, existing, { saveLineage })
   216	      : refreshGraphMetadataForSpecFolder(specFolderPath, { saveLineage }).metadata;
   217	
   218	    if (!existing) {
   219	      summary.created += 1;
   220	    } else {
   221	      summary.refreshed += 1;
   222	      if (!dryRun && !existing.derived.save_lineage && metadata.derived.save_lineage === 'graph_only') {
   223	        summary.lineageStamped += 1;
   224	      }
   225	    }
   226	
   227	    const flags = collectReviewFlags(specFolderPath, metadata);
   228	    if (flags.length > 0) {
   229	      summary.reviewFlags.push({
   230	        specFolder: metadata.spec_folder,

 succeeded in 0ms:
   430	}
   431	
   432	function getPacketIdFromGraphMetadata(specFolderPath: string): string {
   433	  const graphFile = path.join(specFolderPath, GRAPH_METADATA_FILE);
   434	  try {
   435	    const metadata = readGraphMetadata(graphFile);
   436	    if (typeof metadata.packet_id === 'string' && metadata.packet_id.trim().length > 0) {
   437	      return metadata.packet_id;
   438	    }
   439	  } catch {
   440	    // Fall through to path-derived packet id.
   441	  }
   442	
   443	  for (const specsDir of getSpecsDirectories()) {
   444	    const relative = path.relative(path.resolve(specsDir), path.resolve(specFolderPath));
   445	    if (relative && !relative.startsWith('..') && !path.isAbsolute(relative)) {
   446	      return relative.replace(/\\/g, '/');
   447	    }
   448	  }
   449	
   450	  return path.basename(specFolderPath);
   451	}
   452	
   453	export function updatePhaseParentPointer(
   454	  phaseParentPath: string,
   455	  activeChildId: string | null,
   456	  timestamp: string,
   457	): void {
   458	  const graphFile = path.join(phaseParentPath, GRAPH_METADATA_FILE);
   459	  const metadata = readGraphMetadata(graphFile);
   460	  const derived = (
   461	    typeof metadata.derived === 'object' &&
   462	    metadata.derived !== null &&
   463	    !Array.isArray(metadata.derived)
   464	  )
   465	    ? { ...(metadata.derived as Record<string, unknown>) }
   466	    : {};
   467	
   468	  metadata.derived = {
   469	    ...derived,
   470	    last_active_child_id: activeChildId,
   471	    last_active_at: timestamp,
   472	    // Bump parent's last_save_at when the bubble-up touches it,
   473	    // so consumers reading derived metadata see a current save timestamp.
   474	    last_save_at: timestamp,
   475	  };
   476	
   477	  // Ensure children_ids contains the saved child's packet id
   478	  // (idempotent — only inserts when absent so concurrent saves stay safe).
   479	  if (activeChildId) {
   480	    const existingChildren = Array.isArray(metadata.children_ids)
   481	      ? (metadata.children_ids as unknown[]).filter(
   482	          (item): item is string => typeof item === 'string',
   483	        )
   484	      : [];
   485	    if (!existingChildren.includes(activeChildId)) {
   486	      metadata.children_ids = [...existingChildren, activeChildId];
   487	    }
   488	  }
   489	
   490	  atomicWriteJson(graphFile, metadata);

  1316	
  1317	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1318	   7. DATABASE UTILITIES
  1319	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
  1320	
  1321	/**
  1322	 * Closes the shared vector-index database connection.
  1323	 * @returns Nothing.
  1324	 */
  1325	export function close_db(): void {
  1326	  clear_prepared_statements();
  1327	  // C1 FIX: Close all tracked connections
  1328	  for (const [, conn] of db_connections) {
  1329	    try {
  1330	      if (conn !== db) {
  1331	        // Non-active tracked connections must also flush + TRUNCATE their
  1332	        // WAL before close, exactly like the active db below — otherwise a non-active connection
  1333	        // closes with only better-sqlite3's passive checkpoint and can leave un-checkpointed
  1334	        // frames that an abrupt later kill could corrupt (the corruption window).
  1335	        // Checkpoint BEFORE detaching the shard (the shard schema must still be attached).
  1336	        try { conn.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`); } catch (_: unknown) { /* best-effort: shard may not be attached on this conn */ }
  1337	        try { conn.pragma('wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)'); } catch (_: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
  1338	        detachActiveVectorShard(conn);
  1339	        conn.close();
  1340	      }
  1341	    } catch (_: unknown) { /* ignore close errors */ }
  1342	  }
  1343	  db_connections.clear();
  1344	  if (db) {
  1345	    const closing_db_path = db_path;
  1346	    // FTS-corruption prevention: flush + TRUNCATE the
  1347	    // WAL before close so context-index.sqlite is consistent at rest with an empty
  1348	    // WAL. better-sqlite3 `.close()` only does a passive checkpoint and can leave
  1349	    // un-checkpointed frames; an explicit TRUNCATE shrinks the window that an
  1350	    // abrupt later kill (e.g. SIGKILL on MCP reconnect) could corrupt — notably
  1351	    // FTS5 segment writes. Best-effort: a checkpoint failure must never block close.
  1352	    try { db.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`); } catch (_: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
  1353	    let main_checkpoint_succeeded = false;
  1354	    try {
  1355	      db.pragma('wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)');
  1356	      main_checkpoint_succeeded = true;
  1357	    } catch (_: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
  1358	    // DR-011: The clean-shutdown marker semantic is "present == dirty" (it is written
  1359	    // on every open in initialize_db and removed only on a clean close). It must be
  1360	    // deleted ONLY after a confirmed-successful close. detachActiveVectorShard() and
  1361	    // db.close() can both throw; if the marker were removed before they ran (the prior
  1362	    // ordering) and one of them threw, the marker would be gone while the DB did NOT
  1363	    // close cleanly — so the next open would wrongly trust a dirty DB. Detach + close
  1364	    // first (if either throws we never reach the removal, so the marker survives), then
  1365	    // remove the marker inside the checkpoint-success guard.
  1366	    detachActiveVectorShard(db);
  1367	    db.close();
  1368	    if (main_checkpoint_succeeded) {
  1369	      remove_unclean_shutdown_marker(closing_db_path);
  1370	    }
  1371	    db = null;
  1372	  }

+# Iteration 003 — SQ4 launcher-swap common cause + SQ3 graph-churn recheck
+
+## Question
+
+Can SQ4 move from hypothesis to confirmed or refuted? Specifically, does the `mk-spec-memory` launcher lease/respawn path allow a second launcher to terminate the incumbent `context-server` child without a verified DB clean-close barrier, and do the 031/009 fixes close that live two-launcher gap? Also, does SQ3's graph-metadata churn root cause still hold at HEAD, and does it share the daemon-swap lifecycle?
+
+## Investigation (commands run + what each showed)
+
+- `nl -ba .../deep-research-strategy.md`: confirmed SQ4 is P0, SQ3 is P1, and the charter asks whether SQ1/SQ2 share a single-writer / daemon-lifecycle contention cause `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:12`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:13`.

+- `nl -ba .../iteration-001.md` and `nl -ba .../iteration-002.md`: confirmed prior work answered SQ1 as a diagnostic-row failure and left SQ4 narrowed but open around launcher/daemon lifecycle `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/iteration-001.md:22`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/iteration-002.md:48`.

+- `nl -ba .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts` and `nl -ba .../vector-index-store.ts`: traced the graceful close path and the dirty-marker invariant. `SIGTERM` enters `fatalShutdown`, but the close is inside a 5s cleanup race; `.unclean-shutdown` is removed only after main WAL checkpoint, shard detach, and `db.close()` succeed `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1491`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1507`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1528`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1353`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`.

+
+## Findings (each: CONFIRMED|HYPOTHESIS | the claim | evidence file:line | confidence 0-1 | tag fullyNew|partiallyNew|known)
+
+- CONFIRMED | A healthy live lease holder is not clobbered by default: strict single-writer mode checks `isLeaseHeld()`, and if the lease is live it calls `bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld()` and returns before `launchServer()`. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:878`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:880`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:881`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:882`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:883`. Confidence 0.97. Tag partiallyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The second launcher can enter respawn against a live launcher lease when the lease holder's IPC socket fails the deep JSON-RPC probe: `probeDaemon(... deepProbe: true)` must see an initialize reply, and `maybeBridgeLeaseHolder()` returns `action: respawn` when the probe is not alive. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:126`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:164`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:191`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:346`. Confidence 0.96. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The respawn path serializes with bootstrap + respawn locks and re-validates the lease, but those gates protect against duplicate respawners; they do not verify DB closure. After the gates, the path reaps the recorded `childPid`, writes a new lease, and launches a replacement. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:321`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:322`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:329`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:330`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:336`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`. Confidence 0.94. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The second launcher is permitted to terminate the incumbent `context-server` child: `reapLeaseChildBeforeRespawn()` sends `SIGTERM`, waits `RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS`, escalates to `SIGKILL` if the child has not exited, then returns `child-reaped`. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:283`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:292`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:293`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:295`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:297`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:298`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:301`; grace constant: `.opencode/bin/lib/model-server-supervision.cjs:19`. Confidence 0.98. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | There is no clean-close barrier in the two-launcher respawn path. The DB marker is removed only after a successful main WAL checkpoint, detach, and `db.close()`, but the respawn path proceeds after process exit/SIGKILL and does not inspect `.unclean-shutdown`, WAL state, or `close_db()` success. Evidence for the required clean-close invariant: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1353`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1366`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1367`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1369`; evidence for respawn proceeding directly to replacement launch: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:347`. Confidence 0.92. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | `SIGTERM` normally attempts the right DB cleanup, but it is time-bounded rather than barrier-verified: `fatalShutdown()` runs `vectorIndex.closeDb()` inside `cleanup`, races cleanup against `SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_MS`, logs on timeout, and calls `process.exit(exitCode)` either way. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1473`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1491`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1501`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1507`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1528`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1539`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1543`, signal hook: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1596`. Confidence 0.95. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | SQ4's common-cause mechanism is confirmed at code level: a wedged/dead-probed incumbent after DB open can produce both a client-side `Connection closed`/no-reply symptom and a skipped clean DB close, because the bridge probe classifies no initialize reply as dead and the respawn kill path has no DB-close verification barrier. Evidence: SQ1 diagnostic producer `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/stress_test/substrate/run-substrate-stress-harness.mjs:326`, captured diagnostic `_sandbox/24--local-llm-query-intelligence/evidence/run-2026-05-14-shared-daemon.summary.tsv:2`, deep-probe dead verdict `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:202`, respawn kill path `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:297`, DB marker removal condition `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`. Confidence 0.86. Tag fullyNew.
+- HYPOTHESIS | The exact historical SQ1/SQ2 recurrence was this live two-launcher swap rather than another cleanup-bypass exit. Code proves the timing is permitted, but this iteration did not find a timestamped launcher log tying the captured TSV `Connection closed` to a specific `dead-socket` respawn and subsequent `.unclean-shutdown` recurrence. Evidence for permitted path: `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:336`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`; evidence that other cleanup-bypass exits also exist: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1646`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1649`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:2070`. Confidence 0.7. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | 031/009's fixes reduce adjacent risks but do not close the live `mk-spec-memory` two-launcher DB-close gap. The packet says the implemented coverage was deterministic, not live, and the current `mk-spec-memory` lease suite that would exercise real launcher processes is skipped. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:55`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:173`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:174`. Confidence 0.91. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | SQ3's committed HEAD fix holds for the idempotency + pointer-preservation portions: the schema declares `last_active_child_id` / `last_active_at`, derive and merge preserve them, and no-op refresh skips a write when only volatile fields would differ. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:57`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:58`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1096`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1098`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1135`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1170`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1258`; regression tests: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/graph-metadata-refresh.vitest.ts:131`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/graph-metadata-refresh.vitest.ts:148`. Confidence 0.96. Tag fullyNew.

Confidence 0.93. Tag fullyNew.
+
+## Negative knowledge (directions ruled OUT this iteration, with why)
+
+- Ruled out "any second launcher can kill a healthy incumbent." Strict single-writer mode returns after bridge/report for a live lease holder; the kill path is gated on failed IPC liveness, not mere concurrency. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:880`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:882`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`.
+- Ruled out "respawn locks prove clean DB handoff." The locks serialize respawn ownership, but the only code-level clean marker removal is in `close_db()` after checkpoint/close; the respawn path writes a new lease and launches after reaping without checking that marker condition. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:321`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:322`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`.
+- Ruled out "031/009 already proves the live swap is safe." Its own implementation summary says tests are deterministic rather than live, and the real-process `mk-spec-memory` launcher lease suite is skipped. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:173`.
+- Ruled out "SQ3 graph churn is another daemon lifecycle symptom." The graph refresh is a direct metadata write path in the canonical save workflow; its fixes are schema/merge/idempotency/scoping mechanics, not `mk-spec-memory` daemon respawn mechanics. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1705`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1258`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:57`.
+

+# Iteration 003 — SQ4 launcher-swap common cause + SQ3 graph-churn recheck
+
+## Question
+
+Can SQ4 move from hypothesis to confirmed or refuted? Specifically, does the `mk-spec-memory` launcher lease/respawn path allow a second launcher to terminate the incumbent `context-server` child without a verified DB clean-close barrier, and do the 031/009 fixes close that live two-launcher gap? Also, does SQ3's graph-metadata churn root cause still hold at HEAD, and does it share the daemon-swap lifecycle?
+
+## Investigation (commands run + what each showed)
+
+- `nl -ba .../deep-research-strategy.md`: confirmed SQ4 is P0, SQ3 is P1, and the charter asks whether SQ1/SQ2 share a single-writer / daemon-lifecycle contention cause `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:12`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:13`.

+- `nl -ba .../iteration-001.md` and `nl -ba .../iteration-002.md`: confirmed prior work answered SQ1 as a diagnostic-row failure and left SQ4 narrowed but open around launcher/daemon lifecycle `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/iteration-001.md:22`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/iteration-002.md:48`.

It landed deterministic fixes, but its own limitation says live two-launcher integration remained unproven `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:47`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`.

+## Findings (each: CONFIRMED|HYPOTHESIS | the claim | evidence file:line | confidence 0-1 | tag fullyNew|partiallyNew|known)
+
+- CONFIRMED | A healthy live lease holder is not clobbered by default: strict single-writer mode checks `isLeaseHeld()`, and if the lease is live it calls `bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld()` and returns before `launchServer()`. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:878`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:880`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:881`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:882`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:883`. Confidence 0.97. Tag partiallyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The second launcher can enter respawn against a live launcher lease when the lease holder's IPC socket fails the deep JSON-RPC probe: `probeDaemon(... deepProbe: true)` must see an initialize reply, and `maybeBridgeLeaseHolder()` returns `action: respawn` when the probe is not alive. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:126`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:164`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:191`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:346`. Confidence 0.96. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The respawn path serializes with bootstrap + respawn locks and re-validates the lease, but those gates protect against duplicate respawners; they do not verify DB closure. After the gates, the path reaps the recorded `childPid`, writes a new lease, and launches a replacement. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:321`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:322`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:329`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:330`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:336`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`. Confidence 0.94. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The second launcher is permitted to terminate the incumbent `context-server` child: `reapLeaseChildBeforeRespawn()` sends `SIGTERM`, waits `RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS`, escalates to `SIGKILL` if the child has not exited, then returns `child-reaped`. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:283`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:292`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:293`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:295`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:297`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:298`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:301`; grace constant: `.opencode/bin/lib/model-server-supervision.cjs:19`. Confidence 0.98. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | There is no clean-close barrier in the two-launcher respawn path. The DB marker is removed only after a successful main WAL checkpoint, detach, and `db.close()`, but the respawn path proceeds after process exit/SIGKILL and does not inspect `.unclean-shutdown`, WAL state, or `close_db()` success. Evidence for the required clean-close invariant: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1353`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1366`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1367`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1369`; evidence for respawn proceeding directly to replacement launch: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:347`. Confidence 0.92. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | `SIGTERM` normally attempts the right DB cleanup, but it is time-bounded rather than barrier-verified: `fatalShutdown()` runs `vectorIndex.closeDb()` inside `cleanup`, races cleanup against `SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_MS`, logs on timeout, and calls `process.exit(exitCode)` either way. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1473`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1491`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1501`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1507`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1528`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1539`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1543`, signal hook: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1596`. Confidence 0.95. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | SQ4's common-cause mechanism is confirmed at code level: a wedged/dead-probed incumbent after DB open can produce both a client-side `Connection closed`/no-reply symptom and a skipped clean DB close, because the bridge probe classifies no initialize reply as dead and the respawn kill path has no DB-close verification barrier. Evidence: SQ1 diagnostic producer `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/stress_test/substrate/run-substrate-stress-harness.mjs:326`, captured diagnostic `_sandbox/24--local-llm-query-intelligence/evidence/run-2026-05-14-shared-daemon.summary.tsv:2`, deep-probe dead verdict `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:202`, respawn kill path `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:297`, DB marker removal condition `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`. +- CONFIRMED | SQ3 does not share the daemon-swap lifecycle. The canonical save workflow refreshes graph metadata directly for `validatedSpecFolderPath` before semantic indexing, while the explicit backfill tool remains the broad walker when invoked with its default root. Evidence for scoped canonical refresh: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1679`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1684`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1701`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1705`; evidence broad backfill is explicit/default-root behavior: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:7`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:8`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:70`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:72`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:193`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:194`. Confidence 0.93. Tag fullyNew.
+
+## Negative knowledge (directions ruled OUT this iteration, with why)
+
+- Ruled out "any second launcher can kill a healthy incumbent." Strict single-writer mode returns after bridge/report for a live lease holder; the kill path is gated on failed IPC liveness, not mere concurrency. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:880`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:882`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`.
+- Ruled out "respawn locks prove clean DB handoff." The locks serialize respawn ownership, but the only code-level clean marker removal is in `close_db()` after checkpoint/close; the respawn path writes a new lease and launches after reaping without checking that marker condition. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:321`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:322`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`.
+- Ruled out "031/009 already proves the live swap is safe." Its own implementation summary says tests are deterministic rather than live, and the real-process `mk-spec-memory` launcher lease suite is skipped. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:173`.
+- Ruled out "SQ3 graph churn is another daemon lifecycle symptom." The graph refresh is a direct metadata write path in the canonical save workflow; its fixes are schema/merge/idempotency/scoping mechanics, not `mk-spec-memory` daemon respawn mechanics. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1705`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1258`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:57`.
+
+## Open questions remaining (which SQ still unanswered)
+

+- SQ1 is answered by Iteration 001, SQ3 is re-verified here, and SQ4 is answered as a confirmed conditional mechanism with one remaining historical-log caveat `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/iteration-001.md:22`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:12`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:13`.

+# Iteration 003 — SQ4 launcher-swap common cause + SQ3 graph-churn recheck
+
+## Question
+
+Can SQ4 move from hypothesis to confirmed or refuted? Specifically, does the `mk-spec-memory` launcher lease/respawn path allow a second launcher to terminate the incumbent `context-server` child without a verified DB clean-close barrier, and do the 031/009 fixes close that live two-launcher gap? Also, does SQ3's graph-metadata churn root cause still hold at HEAD, and does it share the daemon-swap lifecycle?
+

+
+- `nl -ba .../deep-research-strategy.md`: confirmed SQ4 is P0, SQ3 is P1, and the charter asks whether SQ1/SQ2 share a single-writer / daemon-lifecycle contention cause `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:12`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:13`.

+- `nl -ba .../iteration-001.md` and `nl -ba .../iteration-002.md`: confirmed prior work answered SQ1 as a diagnostic-row failure and left SQ4 narrowed but open around launcher/daemon lifecycle `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/iteration-001.md:22`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/iteration-002.md:48`.

+## Findings (each: CONFIRMED|HYPOTHESIS | the claim | evidence file:line | confidence 0-1 | tag fullyNew|partiallyNew|known)
+
+- CONFIRMED | A healthy live lease holder is not clobbered by default: strict single-writer mode checks `isLeaseHeld()`, and if the lease is live it calls `bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld()` and returns before `launchServer()`. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:878`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:880`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:881`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:882`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:883`. Confidence 0.97. Tag partiallyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The second launcher can enter respawn against a live launcher lease when the lease holder's IPC socket fails the deep JSON-RPC probe: `probeDaemon(... deepProbe: true)` must see an initialize reply, and `maybeBridgeLeaseHolder()` returns `action: respawn` when the probe is not alive. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:126`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:164`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:191`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:346`. Confidence 0.96. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The respawn path serializes with bootstrap + respawn locks and re-validates the lease, but those gates protect against duplicate respawners; they do not verify DB closure. After the gates, the path reaps the recorded `childPid`, writes a new lease, and launches a replacement. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:321`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:322`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:329`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:330`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:336`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`. Confidence 0.94. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The second launcher is permitted to terminate the incumbent `context-server` child: `reapLeaseChildBeforeRespawn()` sends `SIGTERM`, waits `RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS`, escalates to `SIGKILL` if the child has not exited, then returns `child-reaped`. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:283`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:292`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:293`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:295`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:297`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:298`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:301`; grace constant: `.opencode/bin/lib/model-server-supervision.cjs:19`. Confidence 0.98. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | There is no clean-close barrier in the two-launcher respawn path. The DB marker is removed only after a successful main WAL checkpoint, detach, and `db.close()`, but the respawn path proceeds after process exit/SIGKILL and does not inspect `.unclean-shutdown`, WAL state, or `close_db()` success. Evidence for the required clean-close invariant: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1353`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1366`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1367`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1369`; evidence for respawn proceeding directly to replacement launch: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:347`. Confidence 0.92. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | `SIGTERM` normally attempts the right DB cleanup, but it is time-bounded rather than barrier-verified: `fatalShutdown()` runs `vectorIndex.closeDb()` inside `cleanup`, races cleanup against `SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_MS`, logs on timeout, and calls `process.exit(exitCode)` either way. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1473`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1491`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1501`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1507`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1528`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1539`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1543`, signal hook: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1596`. Confidence 0.95. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | SQ4's common-cause mechanism is confirmed at code level: a wedged/dead-probed incumbent after DB open can produce both a client-side `Connection closed`/no-reply symptom and a skipped clean DB close, because the bridge probe classifies no initialize reply as dead and the respawn kill path has no DB-close verification barrier. Evidence: SQ1 diagnostic producer `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/stress_test/substrate/run-substrate-stress-harness.mjs:326`, captured diagnostic `_sandbox/24--local-llm-query-intelligence/evidence/run-2026-05-14-shared-daemon.summary.tsv:2`, deep-probe dead verdict `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:202`, respawn kill path `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:297`, DB marker removal condition `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`. Confidence 0.86. Tag fullyNew.
+- HYPOTHESIS | The exact historical SQ1/SQ2 recurrence was this live two-launcher swap rather than another cleanup-bypass exit. Code proves the timing is permitted, but this iteration did not find a timestamped launcher log tying the captured TSV `Connection closed` to a specific `dead-socket` respawn and subsequent `.unclean-shutdown` recurrence. Evidence for permitted path: `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:336`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`; evidence that other cleanup-bypass exits also exist: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1646`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1649`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:2070`. Confidence 0.7. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | 031/009's fixes reduce adjacent risks but do not close the live `mk-spec-memory` two-launcher DB-close gap. The packet says the implemented coverage was deterministic, not live, and the current `mk-spec-memory` lease suite that would exercise real launcher processes is skipped. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:55`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:173`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:174`. +- CONFIRMED | SQ3's committed HEAD fix holds for the idempotency + pointer-preservation portions: the schema declares `last_active_child_id` / `last_active_at`, derive and merge preserve them, and no-op refresh skips a write when only volatile fields would differ. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:57`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:58`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1096`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1098`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1135`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1170`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1258`; regression tests: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/graph-metadata-refresh.vitest.ts:131`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/graph-metadata-refresh.vitest.ts:148`. +## Open questions remaining (which SQ still unanswered)

+# Iteration 003 — SQ4 launcher-swap common cause + SQ3 graph-churn recheck
+
+## Question
+
+Can SQ4 move from hypothesis to confirmed or refuted? Specifically, does the `mk-spec-memory` launcher lease/respawn path allow a second launcher to terminate the incumbent `context-server` child without a verified DB clean-close barrier, and do the 031/009 fixes close that live two-launcher gap? Also, does SQ3's graph-metadata churn root cause still hold at HEAD, and does it share the daemon-swap lifecycle?
+
