+## Findings (each: CONFIRMED|HYPOTHESIS | the claim | evidence file:line | confidence 0-1 | tag fullyNew|partiallyNew|known)
+
+- CONFIRMED | A healthy live lease holder is not clobbered by default: strict single-writer mode checks `isLeaseHeld()`, and if the lease is live it calls `bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld()` and returns before `launchServer()`. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:878`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:880`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:881`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:882`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:883`. Confidence 0.97. Tag partiallyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The second launcher can enter respawn against a live launcher lease when the lease holder's IPC socket fails the deep JSON-RPC probe: `probeDaemon(... deepProbe: true)` must see an initialize reply, and `maybeBridgeLeaseHolder()` returns `action: respawn` when the probe is not alive. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:126`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:164`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:191`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:346`. Confidence 0.96. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The respawn path serializes with bootstrap + respawn locks and re-validates the lease, but those gates protect against duplicate respawners; they do not verify DB closure. After the gates, the path reaps the recorded `childPid`, writes a new lease, and launches a replacement. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:321`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:322`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:329`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:330`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:336`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`. Confidence 0.94. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The second launcher is permitted to terminate the incumbent `context-server` child: `reapLeaseChildBeforeRespawn()` sends `SIGTERM`, waits `RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS`, escalates to `SIGKILL` if the child has not exited, then returns `child-reaped`. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:283`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:292`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:293`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:295`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:297`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:298`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:301`; grace constant: `.opencode/bin/lib/model-server-supervision.cjs:19`. Confidence 0.98. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | There is no clean-close barrier in the two-launcher respawn path. The DB marker is removed only after a successful main WAL checkpoint, detach, and `db.close()`, but the respawn path proceeds after process exit/SIGKILL and does not inspect `.unclean-shutdown`, WAL state, or `close_db()` success. Evidence for the required clean-close invariant: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1353`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1366`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1367`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1369`; evidence for respawn proceeding directly to replacement launch: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:347`. Confidence 0.92. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | `SIGTERM` normally attempts the right DB cleanup, but it is time-bounded rather than barrier-verified: `fatalShutdown()` runs `vectorIndex.closeDb()` inside `cleanup`, races cleanup against `SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_MS`, logs on timeout, and calls `process.exit(exitCode)` either way. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1473`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1491`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1501`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1507`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1528`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1539`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1543`, signal hook: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1596`. Confidence 0.95. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | SQ4's common-cause mechanism is confirmed at code level: a wedged/dead-probed incumbent after DB open can produce both a client-side `Connection closed`/no-reply symptom and a skipped clean DB close, because the bridge probe classifies no initialize reply as dead and the respawn kill path has no DB-close verification barrier. Evidence: SQ1 diagnostic producer `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/stress_test/substrate/run-substrate-stress-harness.mjs:326`, captured diagnostic `_sandbox/24--local-llm-query-intelligence/evidence/run-2026-05-14-shared-daemon.summary.tsv:2`, deep-probe dead verdict `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:202`, respawn kill path `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:297`, DB marker removal condition `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`. Confidence 0.86. Tag fullyNew.
+- HYPOTHESIS | The exact historical SQ1/SQ2 recurrence was this live two-launcher swap rather than another cleanup-bypass exit. Code proves the timing is permitted, but this iteration did not find a timestamped launcher log tying the captured TSV `Connection closed` to a specific `dead-socket` respawn and subsequent `.unclean-shutdown` recurrence. Evidence for permitted path: `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:336`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`; evidence that other cleanup-bypass exits also exist: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1646`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1649`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:2070`. Confidence 0.7. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | 031/009's fixes reduce adjacent risks but do not close the live `mk-spec-memory` two-launcher DB-close gap. The packet says the implemented coverage was deterministic, not live, and the current `mk-spec-memory` lease suite that would exercise real launcher processes is skipped. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:55`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:173`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:174`. Confidence 0.91. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | SQ3's committed HEAD fix holds for the idempotency + pointer-preservation portions: the schema declares `last_active_child_id` / `last_active_at`, derive and merge preserve them, and no-op refresh skips a write when only volatile fields would differ. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:57`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:58`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1096`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1098`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1135`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1170`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1258`; regression tests: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/graph-metadata-refresh.vitest.ts:131`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/graph-metadata-refresh.vitest.ts:148`. +## Negative knowledge (directions ruled OUT this iteration, with why)
+
+- Ruled out "any second launcher can kill a healthy incumbent." Strict single-writer mode returns after bridge/report for a live lease holder; the kill path is gated on failed IPC liveness, not mere concurrency. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:880`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:882`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`.
+- Ruled out "respawn locks prove clean DB handoff." The locks serialize respawn ownership, but the only code-level clean marker removal is in `close_db()` after checkpoint/close; the respawn path writes a new lease and launches after reaping without checking that marker condition. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:321`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:322`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`.
+- Ruled out "031/009 already proves the live swap is safe." Its own implementation summary says tests are deterministic rather than live, and the real-process `mk-spec-memory` launcher lease suite is skipped. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:173`.
+- Ruled out "SQ3 graph churn is another daemon lifecycle symptom." The graph refresh is a direct metadata write path in the canonical save workflow; its fixes are schema/merge/idempotency/scoping mechanics, not `mk-spec-memory` daemon respawn mechanics. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1705`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1258`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:57`.

+# Iteration 003 — SQ4 launcher-swap common cause + SQ3 graph-churn recheck
+
+## Question
+
+Can SQ4 move from hypothesis to confirmed or refuted? Specifically, does the `mk-spec-memory` launcher lease/respawn path allow a second launcher to terminate the incumbent `context-server` child without a verified DB clean-close barrier, and do the 031/009 fixes close that live two-launcher gap? Also, does SQ3's graph-metadata churn root cause still hold at HEAD, and does it share the daemon-swap lifecycle?
+
+## Investigation (commands run + what each showed)
+
+- `nl -ba .../deep-research-strategy.md`: confirmed SQ4 is P0, SQ3 is P1, and the charter asks whether SQ1/SQ2 share a single-writer / daemon-lifecycle contention cause `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:12`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:13`.
+- `nl -ba .../deep-research-findings-registry.json`: confirmed the registry still contains no reducer-owned findings to deduplicate against `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-findings-registry.json:1`.
+- `nl -ba .../iteration-001.md` and `nl -ba .../iteration-002.md`: confirmed prior work answered SQ1 as a diagnostic-row failure and left SQ4 narrowed but open around launcher/daemon lifecycle `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/iteration-001.md:22`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/iteration-002.md:48`.
+- `rg -n "acquire|lease|SIGKILL|SIGTERM|reap|childPid|spawn|exit" .opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs` plus targeted `nl -ba`: traced lease detection, bridge-or-report behavior, dead-socket respawn locking, child reaping, child launch, and signal shutdown `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:229`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:283`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:321`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:878`.
+- `nl -ba .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs`: confirmed the second launcher uses a deep JSON-RPC initialize probe; a live reply bridges, while timeout/error/closed-before-reply returns `action: respawn` `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:123`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:164`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:191`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:343`.
+- `nl -ba .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts` and `nl -ba .../vector-index-store.ts`: traced the graceful close path and the dirty-marker invariant. `SIGTERM` enters `fatalShutdown`, but the close is inside a 5s cleanup race; `.unclean-shutdown` is removed only after main WAL checkpoint, shard detach, and `db.close()` succeed `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1491`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1507`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1528`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1353`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`.
+- `rg -n "respawn-lock|reap-root|marker-after-close|two-launcher|live" .../031...` plus targeted `nl -ba`: checked 031/009's coverage. It landed deterministic fixes, but its own limitation says live two-launcher integration remained unproven `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:47`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`.
+- `rg -n "mk-spec-memory-launcher|dead-socket|lease holder|bridge|reapLeaseChildBeforeRespawn" .../tests`: checked test coverage. The `mk-spec-memory` lease lifecycle suite is skipped, while the bridge probe tests cover probe decisions and model-server cross-launcher tests cover model-server reaping, not a live context-server DB close barrier `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:173`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-ipc-bridge-probe.vitest.ts:200`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/embedders/launcher-model-server-cross-launcher.vitest.ts:273`.
+- `rg -n "last_save_at|last_active_child_id|last_active_at|refreshGraphMetadataForSpecFolder|backfill" ...`: rechecked SQ3 at HEAD. The parser now preserves chronology fields, skips no-op timestamp rewrites, and the canonical save workflow refreshes `validatedSpecFolderPath` directly `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:57`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1258`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1705`.

+## Findings (each: CONFIRMED|HYPOTHESIS | the claim | evidence file:line | confidence 0-1 | tag fullyNew|partiallyNew|known)
+
+- CONFIRMED | A healthy live lease holder is not clobbered by default: strict single-writer mode checks `isLeaseHeld()`, and if the lease is live it calls `bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld()` and returns before `launchServer()`. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:878`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:880`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:881`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:882`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:883`. Confidence 0.97. Tag partiallyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The second launcher can enter respawn against a live launcher lease when the lease holder's IPC socket fails the deep JSON-RPC probe: `probeDaemon(... deepProbe: true)` must see an initialize reply, and `maybeBridgeLeaseHolder()` returns `action: respawn` when the probe is not alive. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:126`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:164`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:191`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:346`. Confidence 0.96. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The respawn path serializes with bootstrap + respawn locks and re-validates the lease, but those gates protect against duplicate respawners; they do not verify DB closure. After the gates, the path reaps the recorded `childPid`, writes a new lease, and launches a replacement. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:321`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:322`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:329`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:330`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:336`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`. Confidence 0.94. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The second launcher is permitted to terminate the incumbent `context-server` child: `reapLeaseChildBeforeRespawn()` sends `SIGTERM`, waits `RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS`, escalates to `SIGKILL` if the child has not exited, then returns `child-reaped`. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:283`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:292`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:293`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:295`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:297`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:298`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:301`; grace constant: `.opencode/bin/lib/model-server-supervision.cjs:19`. Confidence 0.98. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | There is no clean-close barrier in the two-launcher respawn path. The DB marker is removed only after a successful main WAL checkpoint, detach, and `db.close()`, but the respawn path proceeds after process exit/SIGKILL and does not inspect `.unclean-shutdown`, WAL state, or `close_db()` success. Evidence for the required clean-close invariant: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1353`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1366`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1367`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1369`; evidence for respawn proceeding directly to replacement launch: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:347`. Confidence 0.92. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | `SIGTERM` normally attempts the right DB cleanup, but it is time-bounded rather than barrier-verified: `fatalShutdown()` runs `vectorIndex.closeDb()` inside `cleanup`, races cleanup against `SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_MS`, logs on timeout, and calls `process.exit(exitCode)` either way. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1473`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1491`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1501`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1507`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1528`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1539`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1543`, signal hook: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1596`. Confidence 0.95. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | SQ4's common-cause mechanism is confirmed at code level: a wedged/dead-probed incumbent after DB open can produce both a client-side `Connection closed`/no-reply symptom and a skipped clean DB close, because the bridge probe classifies no initialize reply as dead and the respawn kill path has no DB-close verification barrier. Evidence: SQ1 diagnostic producer `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/stress_test/substrate/run-substrate-stress-harness.mjs:326`, captured diagnostic `_sandbox/24--local-llm-query-intelligence/evidence/run-2026-05-14-shared-daemon.summary.tsv:2`, deep-probe dead verdict `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:202`, respawn kill path `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:297`, DB marker removal condition `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`. Confidence 0.86. Tag fullyNew.
+- HYPOTHESIS | The exact historical SQ1/SQ2 recurrence was this live two-launcher swap rather than another cleanup-bypass exit. Code proves the timing is permitted, but this iteration did not find a timestamped launcher log tying the captured TSV `Connection closed` to a specific `dead-socket` respawn and subsequent `.unclean-shutdown` recurrence. Evidence for permitted path: `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:336`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`; evidence that other cleanup-bypass exits also exist: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1646`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1649`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:2070`. Confidence 0.7. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | 031/009's fixes reduce adjacent risks but do not close the live `mk-spec-memory` two-launcher DB-close gap. The packet says the implemented coverage was deterministic, not live, and the current `mk-spec-memory` lease suite that would exercise real launcher processes is skipped. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:55`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:173`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:174`. Confidence 0.91. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | SQ3's committed HEAD fix holds for the idempotency + pointer-preservation portions: the schema declares `last_active_child_id` / `last_active_at`, derive and merge preserve them, and no-op refresh skips a write when only volatile fields would differ. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:57`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:58`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1096`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1098`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1135`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1170`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1258`; regression tests: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/graph-metadata-refresh.vitest.ts:131`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/graph-metadata-refresh.vitest.ts:148`. Confidence 0.96. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | SQ3 does not share the daemon-swap lifecycle. The canonical save workflow refreshes graph metadata directly for `validatedSpecFolderPath` before semantic indexing, while the explicit backfill tool remains the broad walker when invoked with its default root. Evidence for scoped canonical refresh: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1679`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1684`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1701`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1705`; evidence broad backfill is explicit/default-root behavior: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:7`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:8`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:70`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:72`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:193`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:194`. Confidence 0.93. Tag fullyNew.
+
+## Negative knowledge (directions ruled OUT this iteration, with why)
+
+- Ruled out "any second launcher can kill a healthy incumbent." Strict single-writer mode returns after bridge/report for a live lease holder; the kill path is gated on failed IPC liveness, not mere concurrency. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:880`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:882`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`.
+- Ruled out "respawn locks prove clean DB handoff." The locks serialize respawn ownership, but the only code-level clean marker removal is in `close_db()` after checkpoint/close; the respawn path writes a new lease and launches after reaping without checking that marker condition. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:321`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:322`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`.
+- Ruled out "031/009 already proves the live swap is safe." Its own implementation summary says tests are deterministic rather than live, and the real-process `mk-spec-memory` launcher lease suite is skipped. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:173`.
+- Ruled out "SQ3 graph churn is another daemon lifecycle symptom." The graph refresh is a direct metadata write path in the canonical save workflow; its fixes are schema/merge/idempotency/scoping mechanics, not `mk-spec-memory` daemon respawn mechanics. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1705`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1258`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:57`.
+
+## Open questions remaining (which SQ still unanswered)
+

+- SQ1 is answered by Iteration 001, SQ3 is re-verified here, and SQ4 is answered as a confirmed conditional mechanism with one remaining historical-log caveat `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/iteration-001.md:22`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:12`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/014-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:13`.

+# Iteration 003 — SQ4 launcher-swap common cause + SQ3 graph-churn recheck
+
+## Question
+
+Can SQ4 move from hypothesis to confirmed or refuted? Specifically, does the `mk-spec-memory` launcher lease/respawn path allow a second launcher to terminate the incumbent `context-server` child without a verified DB clean-close barrier, and do the 031/009 fixes close that live two-launcher gap? Also, does SQ3's graph-metadata churn root cause still hold at HEAD, and does it share the daemon-swap lifecycle?

+## Findings (each: CONFIRMED|HYPOTHESIS | the claim | evidence file:line | confidence 0-1 | tag fullyNew|partiallyNew|known)
+
+- CONFIRMED | A healthy live lease holder is not clobbered by default: strict single-writer mode checks `isLeaseHeld()`, and if the lease is live it calls `bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld()` and returns before `launchServer()`. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:878`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:880`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:881`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:882`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:883`. Confidence 0.97. Tag partiallyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The second launcher can enter respawn against a live launcher lease when the lease holder's IPC socket fails the deep JSON-RPC probe: `probeDaemon(... deepProbe: true)` must see an initialize reply, and `maybeBridgeLeaseHolder()` returns `action: respawn` when the probe is not alive. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:126`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:164`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:191`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:346`. Confidence 0.96. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The respawn path serializes with bootstrap + respawn locks and re-validates the lease, but those gates protect against duplicate respawners; they do not verify DB closure. After the gates, the path reaps the recorded `childPid`, writes a new lease, and launches a replacement. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:321`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:322`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:329`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:330`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:336`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`. Confidence 0.94. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The second launcher is permitted to terminate the incumbent `context-server` child: `reapLeaseChildBeforeRespawn()` sends `SIGTERM`, waits `RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS`, escalates to `SIGKILL` if the child has not exited, then returns `child-reaped`. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:283`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:292`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:293`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:295`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:297`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:298`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:301`; grace constant: `.opencode/bin/lib/model-server-supervision.cjs:19`. Confidence 0.98. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | There is no clean-close barrier in the two-launcher respawn path. The DB marker is removed only after a successful main WAL checkpoint, detach, and `db.close()`, but the respawn path proceeds after process exit/SIGKILL and does not inspect `.unclean-shutdown`, WAL state, or `close_db()` success. Evidence for the required clean-close invariant: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1353`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1366`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1367`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1369`; evidence for respawn proceeding directly to replacement launch: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:347`. Confidence 0.92. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | `SIGTERM` normally attempts the right DB cleanup, but it is time-bounded rather than barrier-verified: `fatalShutdown()` runs `vectorIndex.closeDb()` inside `cleanup`, races cleanup against `SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_MS`, logs on timeout, and calls `process.exit(exitCode)` either way. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1473`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1491`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1501`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1507`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1528`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1539`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1543`, signal hook: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1596`. Confidence 0.95. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | SQ4's common-cause mechanism is confirmed at code level: a wedged/dead-probed incumbent after DB open can produce both a client-side `Connection closed`/no-reply symptom and a skipped clean DB close, because the bridge probe classifies no initialize reply as dead and the respawn kill path has no DB-close verification barrier. Evidence: SQ1 diagnostic producer `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/stress_test/substrate/run-substrate-stress-harness.mjs:326`, captured diagnostic `_sandbox/24--local-llm-query-intelligence/evidence/run-2026-05-14-shared-daemon.summary.tsv:2`, deep-probe dead verdict `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:202`, respawn kill path `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:297`, DB marker removal condition `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`. Confidence 0.86. Tag fullyNew.
+- HYPOTHESIS | The exact historical SQ1/SQ2 recurrence was this live two-launcher swap rather than another cleanup-bypass exit. Code proves the timing is permitted, but this iteration did not find a timestamped launcher log tying the captured TSV `Connection closed` to a specific `dead-socket` respawn and subsequent `.unclean-shutdown` recurrence. Evidence for permitted path: `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:336`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`; evidence that other cleanup-bypass exits also exist: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1646`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1649`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:2070`. Confidence 0.7. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | 031/009's fixes reduce adjacent risks but do not close the live `mk-spec-memory` two-launcher DB-close gap. The packet says the implemented coverage was deterministic, not live, and the current `mk-spec-memory` lease suite that would exercise real launcher processes is skipped. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:55`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:173`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:174`. Confidence 0.91. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | SQ3's committed HEAD fix holds for the idempotency + pointer-preservation portions: the schema declares `last_active_child_id` / `last_active_at`, derive and merge preserve them, and no-op refresh skips a write when only volatile fields would differ. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:57`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:58`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1096`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1098`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1135`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1170`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1258`; regression tests: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/graph-metadata-refresh.vitest.ts:131`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/graph-metadata-refresh.vitest.ts:148`. Confidence 0.96. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | SQ3 does not share the daemon-swap lifecycle. The canonical save workflow refreshes graph metadata directly for `validatedSpecFolderPath` before semantic indexing, while the explicit backfill tool remains the broad walker when invoked with its default root. Evidence for scoped canonical refresh: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1679`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1684`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1701`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1705`; evidence broad backfill is explicit/default-root behavior: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:7`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:8`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:70`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:72`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:193`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.ts:194`. Confidence 0.93. Tag fullyNew.

+## Negative knowledge (directions ruled OUT this iteration, with why)
+
+- Ruled out "any second launcher can kill a healthy incumbent." Strict single-writer mode returns after bridge/report for a live lease holder; the kill path is gated on failed IPC liveness, not mere concurrency. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:880`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:882`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:343`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344`.
+- Ruled out "respawn locks prove clean DB handoff." The locks serialize respawn ownership, but the only code-level clean marker removal is in `close_db()` after checkpoint/close; the respawn path writes a new lease and launches after reaping without checking that marker condition. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:321`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:322`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:345`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:346`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`.
+- Ruled out "031/009 already proves the live swap is safe." Its own implementation summary says tests are deterministic rather than live, and the real-process `mk-spec-memory` launcher lease suite is skipped. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/011-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts:173`.
+- Ruled out "SQ3 graph churn is another daemon lifecycle symptom." The graph refresh is a direct metadata write path in the canonical save workflow; its fixes are schema/merge/idempotency/scoping mechanics, not `mk-spec-memory` daemon respawn mechanics. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts:1705`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts:1258`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-schema.ts:57`.
+

Key result: SQ4 is confirmed as a conditional code-level common-cause path. A second launcher cannot clobber a healthy live lease holder, but if the incumbent IPC probe fails, the respawn path can `SIGTERM`/`SIGKILL` the recorded `context-server` child and launch a replacement without verifying the DB clean-close marker. SQ3 was re-verified at HEAD and does not share that daemon-swap lifecycle.
